name: Add app access restrictions
description: Adds GitHub Apps to the list of apps allowed to push to a protected branch. The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled. This endpoint only works for organization repositories, not personal repositories. Apps must be installed on the repository with 'contents' write permissions.

inputs.name: apps
inputs.description: The list of GitHub App slugs with push access to grant. Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permissions. The app slug is the URL-friendly name of the GitHub App (e.g., 'github-actions', 'dependabot').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive. Note: This endpoint only works for organization-owned repositories.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch to which restrictions will be applied. Wildcard characters (e.g., '*') are not allowed in the branch name when using this REST API endpoint. For operations involving wildcard branch names, consult the GitHub GraphQL API.

requiredInputs.name: apps
requiredInputs.description: The list of GitHub App slugs with push access to grant. Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permissions. The app slug is the URL-friendly name of the GitHub App (e.g., 'github-actions', 'dependabot').

name: Add a repository collaborator
description: Adds a GitHub user as a repository collaborator, or updates their permission if already a collaborator; `permission` applies to organization-owned repositories (personal ones default to 'push' and ignore this field), and an invitation may be created or permissions updated directly.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub handle for the user account to add as a collaborator. Cannot be the same as the repository owner, as owners are automatically collaborators.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Permission level for the collaborator. For organization-owned repositories, valid values are `pull` (read-only), `triage` (manage issues/PRs), `push` (read/write), `maintain` (manage most settings), `admin` (full control), or a custom repository role name if defined by the owning organization. This parameter is ignored for personal repositories, which grant `push` access.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: username
requiredInputs.description: The GitHub handle for the user account to add as a collaborator. Cannot be the same as the repository owner, as owners are automatically collaborators.

optionalInputs.name: permission
optionalInputs.description: Permission level for the collaborator. For organization-owned repositories, valid values are `pull` (read-only), `triage` (manage issues/PRs), `push` (read/write), `maintain` (manage most settings), `admin` (full control), or a custom repository role name if defined by the owning organization. This parameter is ignored for personal repositories, which grant `push` access.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Add assignees to an issue
description: Adds assignees to a GitHub issue. This action only adds users - it does not remove existing assignees. Changes are silently ignored if the authenticated user lacks push access to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.description: A list of GitHub usernames to add as assignees to the issue. Up to 10 assignees total can be on an issue. NOTE: This endpoint ADDS assignees - it does not replace existing ones. Users already assigned are kept. To remove assignees, use the 'remove_assignees_from_an_issue' action instead. The authenticated user must have push access to the repository; otherwise changes are silently ignored.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the issue within the repository. Must be a positive integer (GitHub issue numbers start at 1).

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: issue_number
requiredInputs.description: The unique number identifying the issue within the repository. Must be a positive integer (GitHub issue numbers start at 1).

optionalInputs.name: assignees
optionalInputs.description: A list of GitHub usernames to add as assignees to the issue. Up to 10 assignees total can be on an issue. NOTE: This endpoint ADDS assignees - it does not replace existing ones. Users already assigned are kept. To remove assignees, use the 'remove_assignees_from_an_issue' action instead. The authenticated user must have push access to the repository; otherwise changes are silently ignored.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

name: Add or update team project permissions
description: Adds a classic project to a team or updates the team's permission on it. This endpoint grants or updates permissions for a team on a specific classic project (not Projects V2). The authenticated user must have admin permissions for the project. Both the team and project must belong to the same organization. Requirements: - The project must be a classic project (not GitHub Projects V2) - The authenticated user must have admin permissions on the project - The team and project must be in the same organization - Requires 'admin:org' scope for the authentication token Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success, 403 if project is not an org project, or 404 if the organization, team, or project is not found.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (login) that owns both the team and the classic project. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The team's URL-friendly slug identifier within the organization. Found in the team's URL path.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Permission level to grant the team: 'read' (view only), 'write' (view and edit), or 'admin' (full control including settings). If omitted, uses the team's default permission.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: The unique numeric ID of the classic project. This is NOT the project number displayed in the UI.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The organization name (login) that owns both the team and the classic project. Case-insensitive.

optionalInputs.name: permission
optionalInputs.description: Permission level to grant the team: 'read' (view only), 'write' (view and edit), or 'admin' (full control including settings). If omitted, uses the team's default permission.

name: Add status check contexts
description: Adds status check contexts to a protected branch's required status checks. This action appends new status check contexts to the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.description: The name of the branch to which status check contexts will be added. Branch names cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GraphQL API.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: The status check contexts to add to the protected branch. These are the names of CI/CD jobs or other checks that must pass before merging. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings. Examples: 'ci/test', 'linter', 'continuous-integration/travis-ci'.

name: Add team access restrictions
description: Adds teams to the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action grants additional teams push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Set team access restrictions' (PUT), this action adds to the existing list rather than replacing it entirely. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization name that owns the repository. Team access restrictions only work for organization-owned repositories, not personal repositories. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: teams
inputs.description: The list of team slugs to add push access to the protected branch. A team slug is the URL-friendly version of the team name (lowercase, hyphens instead of spaces). The teams must already have access to the repository. This adds teams to the existing list (does not replace the entire list).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the protected branch to add team restrictions to. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: teams

The teams must already have access to the repository. This adds teams to the existing list (does not replace the entire list).

requiredInputs.name: branch
requiredInputs.description: The name of the protected branch to add team restrictions to. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: List Docker Migration Conflicting Packages
description: List Docker packages with migration conflicts for the authenticated user. This endpoint lists all Docker packages owned by the authenticated user that encountered namespace conflicts during the Docker-to-GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) migration. Conflicts occur when a package with the same name exists in both the legacy Docker registry and GHCR. IMPORTANT: The Docker registry for GitHub Packages was deprecated on Feb 24, 2025. This endpoint may return a 400 error with message 'Package migration for docker is no longer supported' as the migration period has ended. In this case, the action returns an informative response instead of failing. Use case: Identifying packages that require manual migration to GHCR. Required scope: read:packages (for OAuth and personal access tokens).

name: Clear repository cache by key
description: Deletes GitHub Actions caches from a repository matching a specific `key` and an optional Git `ref`, used to manage storage or clear outdated/corrupted caches; the action succeeds even if no matching caches are found to delete.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: The exact cache key used to identify specific caches for deletion. This key is often dynamically generated in workflows using expressions (e.g., `${{ runner.os }}-npm-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}`).

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The full Git reference for narrowing down the cache deletion. For example, to target caches for a specific branch, use `refs/heads/<branch_name>`. To target caches for a pull request, use `refs/pull/<pull_number>/merge`. If omitted, caches matching the key across all refs are considered.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

name: Commit Multiple Files
description: Tool to atomically create, update, or delete multiple files in a GitHub repository as a single commit. Uses Git Data APIs to avoid SHA mismatch conflicts that occur with the Contents API when multiple files are modified in parallel. Use when you need to make multi-file changes reliably. BRANCH CREATION: When committing to a new branch (e.g., 'fix/my-fix' or 'feature/new-feature'), you MUST provide 'base_branch' (typically 'main' or 'master') to create the branch from. If the branch already exists, base_branch is not needed. This action handles race conditions automatically: if the branch is updated by another commit between fetching the HEAD and updating the reference (resulting in a 422 'not a fast forward' error), the action will retry by refetching the HEAD and rebasing changes. Use max_retries to control this behavior.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name (without .git extension)

inputs.name: force
inputs.description: Force update the branch reference. WARNING: Use with caution as this can overwrite commits. Only use this when you intentionally want to overwrite the branch history.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Repository owner (username or organization name)

inputs.name: author
inputs.description: Git author or committer information.

inputs.name: date
inputs.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for the commit (optional, defaults to current time)

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the author or committer

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: Email address of the author or committer

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Target branch name to commit to. If this branch doesn't exist, you MUST provide 'base_branch' to create it. For new feature branches (e.g., 'fix/my-fix', 'feature/new-feature'), always set base_branch='main' (or your default branch).

inputs.name: deletes
inputs.description: List of file paths to delete from the repository. Files must exist in the repository; attempting to delete non-existent files will result in an error. Ensure paths are exact with no leading/trailing whitespace.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Commit message describing the changes

inputs.name: upserts
inputs.description: List of files to create or update. Each entry requires path, content, and optional encoding (utf-8 or base64)

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: File path in the repository (e.g., 'src/main.py' or 'docs/readme.md')

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: File content as a string

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Content encoding: 'utf-8' for text files or 'base64' for binary files

inputs.name: committer
inputs.description: Git author or committer information.

inputs.name: date
inputs.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for the commit (optional, defaults to current time)

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the author or committer

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: Email address of the author or committer

inputs.name: base_branch
inputs.description: REQUIRED when committing to a new branch that doesn't exist yet. Specifies the existing branch to create the new branch from (e.g., 'main' or 'master'). Not needed if 'branch' already exists in the repository.

inputs.name: max_retries
inputs.description: Maximum number of retries when encountering race conditions (422 'not a fast forward' errors). The action will refetch the HEAD and rebase changes on each retry. Set to 0 to disable retries.

name: Compare two commits
description: Compares two commit points (commits, branches, tags, or SHAs) within a repository or across forks, using `BASE...HEAD` or `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` format for the `basehead` parameter.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of the results to fetch, for pagination when the number of differences (commits or files) is large.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: basehead
inputs.description: A string specifying the base and head references to compare, in the format `BASE...HEAD`. `BASE` and `HEAD` can be branch names (e.g., `main...develop`), tag names (e.g., `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`), or commit SHAs (abbreviated or full 40-character format). Both abbreviated and full SHAs are accepted; full 40-character SHAs are recommended for reliability in large repositories. Both references must exist in the specified repository. To compare branches across forks in the same network, use the format `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` (e.g., `octocat:main...another-user:main`). Do NOT pass GitHub URLs - only plain reference strings like 'main...develop' are accepted.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results (e.g., files or commits) to return per page, with a maximum of 100, for pagination.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: basehead
requiredInputs.description: A string specifying the base and head references to compare, in the format `BASE...HEAD`. `BASE` and `HEAD` can be branch names (e.g., `main...develop`), tag names (e.g., `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`), or commit SHAs (abbreviated or full 40-character format). Both abbreviated and full SHAs are accepted; full 40-character SHAs are recommended for reliability in large repositories. Both references must exist in the specified repository. To compare branches across forks in the same network, use the format `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` (e.g., `octocat:main...another-user:main`). Do NOT pass GitHub URLs - only plain reference strings like 'main...develop' are accepted.

optionalInputs.name: page
optionalInputs.description: Page number of the results to fetch, for pagination when the number of differences (commits or files) is large.

optionalInputs.name: per_page
optionalInputs.description: Number of results (e.g., files or commits) to return per page, with a maximum of 100, for pagination.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

name: Create a check run
description: Creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository, used by external services to report status, detailed feedback, annotations, and images directly within the GitHub UI. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot create check runs.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the check to be displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, "Code Coverage" or "Linter".

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: The current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `status` to `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

inputs.name: actions
inputs.description: A list of up to three action objects defining interactive buttons displayed on GitHub after the check run completes. Each object needs a `label` (button text), `identifier` (unique ID for the action), and `description` (tooltip). Clicking a button sends a `check_run.requested_action` webhook to your app.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: The text to be displayed on a button in the web UI (max 20 characters).

inputs.name: identifier
inputs.description: A reference for the action on the integrator's system (max 20 characters).

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short explanation of what this action would do (max 40 characters).

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: The SHA of the commit for which the check run is created.

inputs.name: conclusion
inputs.description: **Required if `completed_at` is provided or if `status` is `completed`**. Specifies the final outcome of the check (e.g., 'success', 'failure'). Note: Setting `conclusion` automatically sets `status` to `completed`. GitHub alone can set a conclusion to `stale`.

inputs.name: started_at
inputs.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run began.

inputs.name: details_url
inputs.description: The URL of the integrator's site providing full details of the check. If omitted, the GitHub App's homepage is used. This URL is available from the check's UI.

inputs.name: external_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the check run on the integrator's system. This helps in correlating check runs between GitHub and the external system.

inputs.name: completed_at
inputs.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run finished. **Required if `status` is `completed`**.

inputs.name: output__text
inputs.description: Detailed textual information about the check run's output. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

inputs.name: output__title
inputs.description: A descriptive title for the output of the check run, displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, 'Linter Scan Summary'.

inputs.name: output__images
inputs.description: A list of image objects to display in the check run's output in the GitHub UI. Each object requires `alt` (alternative text), `image_url` (URL of the image), and an optional `caption`.

inputs.name: alt
inputs.description: The alternative text for the image.

inputs.name: caption
inputs.description: A short image description.

inputs.name: image_url
inputs.description: The full URL of the image.

inputs.name: output__summary
inputs.description: A summary of the check run's findings. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

inputs.name: output__annotations
inputs.description: A list of annotation objects detailing issues at specific code locations. Annotations are visible in the 'Checks' and 'Files changed' tabs of a pull request. Each annotation object should specify `path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `annotation_level` ('notice', 'warning', or 'failure'), and `message`. Optional fields include `start_column`, `end_column`, `title`, and `raw_details`. Up to 50 annotations per API request; for more, use subsequent 'Update a check run' calls. GitHub Actions limits are 10 warnings and 10 errors per step.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The path of the file to add an annotation to.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title that represents the annotation.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: A short description of the feedback for these lines of code.

inputs.name: end_line
inputs.description: The end line of the annotation.

inputs.name: end_column
inputs.description: The end column of the annotation. Annotations only support start_column and end_column on the same line.

inputs.name: start_line
inputs.description: The start line of the annotation.

inputs.name: raw_details
inputs.description: Details about this annotation.

inputs.name: start_column
inputs.description: The start column of the annotation. Annotations only support start_column and end_column on the same line.

inputs.name: annotation_level
inputs.description: The level of the annotation (notice, warning, or failure).

requiredInputs.name: name
requiredInputs.description: The name of the check to be displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, "Code Coverage" or "Linter".

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: head_sha
requiredInputs.description: The SHA of the commit for which the check run is created.

optionalInputs.name: status
optionalInputs.description: The current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `status` to `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

optionalInputs.name: actions
optionalInputs.description: A list of up to three action objects defining interactive buttons displayed on GitHub after the check run completes. Each object needs a `label` (button text), `identifier` (unique ID for the action), and `description` (tooltip). Clicking a button sends a `check_run.requested_action` webhook to your app.

optionalInputs.name: label
optionalInputs.description: The text to be displayed on a button in the web UI (max 20 characters).

optionalInputs.name: identifier
optionalInputs.description: A reference for the action on the integrator's system (max 20 characters).

optionalInputs.name: description
optionalInputs.description: A short explanation of what this action would do (max 40 characters).

optionalInputs.name: conclusion
optionalInputs.description: **Required if `completed_at` is provided or if `status` is `completed`**. Specifies the final outcome of the check (e.g., 'success', 'failure'). Note: Setting `conclusion` automatically sets `status` to `completed`. GitHub alone can set a conclusion to `stale`.

optionalInputs.name: started_at
optionalInputs.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run began.

optionalInputs.name: details_url
optionalInputs.description: The URL of the integrator's site providing full details of the check. If omitted, the GitHub App's homepage is used. This URL is available from the check's UI.

optionalInputs.name: external_id
optionalInputs.description: A unique identifier for the check run on the integrator's system. This helps in correlating check runs between GitHub and the external system.

optionalInputs.name: completed_at
optionalInputs.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run finished. **Required if `status` is `completed`**.

optionalInputs.name: output__text
optionalInputs.description: Detailed textual information about the check run's output. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

optionalInputs.name: output__title
optionalInputs.description: A descriptive title for the output of the check run, displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, 'Linter Scan Summary'.

optionalInputs.name: output__images
optionalInputs.description: A list of image objects to display in the check run's output in the GitHub UI. Each object requires `alt` (alternative text), `image_url` (URL of the image), and an optional `caption`.

optionalInputs.name: caption
optionalInputs.description: A short image description.

optionalInputs.name: image_url
optionalInputs.description: The full URL of the image.

optionalInputs.name: output__summary
optionalInputs.description: A summary of the check run's findings. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

name: Create a codespace from a pull request
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for an open pull request in a Codespaces-enabled repository, with options to customize its configuration.

inputs.name: geo
inputs.description: Geographic area for this codespace (e.g., `UsEast`, `EuropeWest`); supersedes `location`. If unspecified, typically IP-derived.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.description: Machine type for this codespace (e.g., `standardLinux`). Varies by CPU, RAM, storage. Uses default if unspecified.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Requested geographic location (e.g., `EastUs`, `WestEurope`). This parameter is deprecated; use `geo`. If unspecified, location may be IP-derived.

inputs.name: client_ip
inputs.description: Client's public IP address for location auto-detection if proxied and `geo` or `location` are not specified.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the pull request.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Custom name for the codespace.

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.description: Path to a `devcontainer.json` file in the repository that defines the development environment.

inputs.name: working_directory
inputs.description: Working directory for the codespace upon opening. Defaults to the repository root if unspecified.

inputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
inputs.description: Minutes of inactivity after which the codespace is automatically stopped. A default timeout applies if unspecified.

inputs.name: retention_period_minutes
inputs.description: Retention period in minutes (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days) for a stopped codespace. 0 means immediate deletion.

inputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
inputs.description: Set to `true` to prevent GitHub from requesting additional repository permissions defined in `devcontainer.json`. Defaults to `false` (permissions will be requested).

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

requiredInputs.name: pull_number
requiredInputs.description: The unique number identifying the pull request.

optionalInputs.name: geo
optionalInputs.description: Geographic area for this codespace (e.g., `UsEast`, `EuropeWest`); supersedes `location`. If unspecified, typically IP-derived.

optionalInputs.name: machine

optionalInputs.name: location
optionalInputs.description: Requested geographic location (e.g., `EastUs`, `WestEurope`). This parameter is deprecated; use `geo`. If unspecified, location may be IP-derived.

optionalInputs.name: client_ip
optionalInputs.description: Client's public IP address for location auto-detection if proxied and `geo` or `location` are not specified.

optionalInputs.name: display_name
optionalInputs.description: Custom name for the codespace.

optionalInputs.name: devcontainer_path
optionalInputs.description: Path to a `devcontainer.json` file in the repository that defines the development environment.

optionalInputs.name: working_directory
optionalInputs.description: Working directory for the codespace upon opening. Defaults to the repository root if unspecified.

optionalInputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
optionalInputs.description: Minutes of inactivity after which the codespace is automatically stopped. A default timeout applies if unspecified.

optionalInputs.name: retention_period_minutes
optionalInputs.description: Retention period in minutes (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days) for a stopped codespace. 0 means immediate deletion.

optionalInputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
optionalInputs.description: Set to `true` to prevent GitHub from requesting additional repository permissions defined in `devcontainer.json`. Defaults to `false` (permissions will be requested).

name: Create a codespace in a repository
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user in a specified repository, which must be accessible and use a valid `devcontainer.json` if `devcontainer_path` is specified.

inputs.name: geo
inputs.description: Geographic area for the codespace, replacing `location`; determined by user IP if not specified.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Git reference (e.g., branch name) for the codespace; uses repository's default branch if not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name owning the repository; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.description: Machine type (CPU, RAM, storage). See 'List available machine types for a repository' action for options; uses a default type if not specified.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Requested AWS region (best-effort, may differ); determined by user IP if not provided. Deprecated: use `geo`.

inputs.name: client_ip
inputs.description: IP address to infer preferred region if `location` and `geo` are unspecified; useful for proxied requests.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Custom display name for the codespace; a default name is generated if not provided.

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.description: Path to a `devcontainer.json` in the repository; uses `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` or `devcontainer.json` at root if not specified.

inputs.name: working_directory
inputs.description: Directory to use as default when codespace opens; uses repository root if not specified.

inputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
inputs.description: Minutes of inactivity before codespace auto-stops; uses a default timeout if not specified.

inputs.name: retention_period_minutes
inputs.description: Minutes an idle (stopped) codespace is retained before auto-deletion (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days); uses a default period if not specified.

inputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
inputs.description: If `true`, opts out of granting multi-repository permissions from `devcontainer.json`. If unspecified or `false`, permissions are granted if requested.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Username or organization name owning the repository; not case-sensitive.

optionalInputs.name: geo
optionalInputs.description: Geographic area for the codespace, replacing `location`; determined by user IP if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: ref
optionalInputs.description: Git reference (e.g., branch name) for the codespace; uses repository's default branch if not provided.

optionalInputs.name: machine
optionalInputs.description: Machine type (CPU, RAM, storage). See 'List available machine types for a repository' action for options; uses a default type if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: location
optionalInputs.description: Requested AWS region (best-effort, may differ); determined by user IP if not provided. Deprecated: use `geo`.

optionalInputs.name: client_ip
optionalInputs.description: IP address to infer preferred region if `location` and `geo` are unspecified; useful for proxied requests.

optionalInputs.name: display_name
optionalInputs.description: Custom display name for the codespace; a default name is generated if not provided.

optionalInputs.name: devcontainer_path
optionalInputs.description: Path to a `devcontainer.json` in the repository; uses `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` or `devcontainer.json` at root if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: working_directory
optionalInputs.description: Directory to use as default when codespace opens; uses repository root if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
optionalInputs.description: Minutes of inactivity before codespace auto-stops; uses a default timeout if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: retention_period_minutes
optionalInputs.description: Minutes an idle (stopped) codespace is retained before auto-deletion (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days); uses a default period if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
optionalInputs.description: If `true`, opts out of granting multi-repository permissions from `devcontainer.json`. If unspecified or `false`, permissions are granted if requested.

outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

name: Create a commit
description: Creates a new commit in a GitHub repository; the `tree` SHA and any `parents` SHAs must already exist in the repository.

inputs.name: tree

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The commit message.

inputs.name: parents
inputs.description: SHAs of parent commits; all must exist in the repository. Omit or provide an empty list for a root commit.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: ASCII-armored detached PGP signature over the commit data. GitHub adds this to the `gpgsig` header if provided.

inputs.name: author__date
inputs.description: Timestamp (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) when the commit was authored.

inputs.name: author__name
inputs.description: Name of the commit's author. Defaults to authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: author__email
inputs.description: Email of the commit's author. Defaults to authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: committer__date
inputs.description: Timestamp (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) when the commit was committed. Defaults to `author_date` or current time.

inputs.name: committer__name
inputs.description: Name of the committer. Defaults to author's name or authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: committer__email
inputs.description: Email of the committer. Defaults to author's email or authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: read_only
inputs.description: If `true`, the deploy key will only have read access. If `false` (default), the key will have read and write access. Write access grants significant privileges.

name: Create a deployment
description: Creates a GitHub deployment for an existing repository, targeting a specific ref (branch, tag, or SHA) that must also exist within the repository.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The ref to deploy. This can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit SHA.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and the authenticated user must have write access to it.

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: Specifies a task to execute, e.g., `deploy` or `deploy:migrations`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and the authenticated user must have access to it.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: A JSON string payload providing additional information that a deployment system might need, customizable based on the deployment process.

inputs.name: auto_merge
inputs.description: Attempts to automatically merge the default branch into the requested ref if the ref is behind the default branch. Set to `false` to disable.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the deployment. Maximum length of 1000 characters.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Name for the target deployment environment (e.g., `production`, `staging`, `qa`).

inputs.name: required_contexts
inputs.description: A list of status check contexts to verify against commit status checks before creating the deployment. If omitted or `null`, GitHub verifies all unique contexts. Pass an empty list `[]` to bypass all checks.

inputs.name: transient_environment

inputs.name: source__path

inputs.name: source__branch

name: Create an autolink reference for a repository

inputs.description: Specifies if the `<num>` placeholder in `url_template` matches alphanumeric characters (`A-Z` (case-insensitive), `0-9`, and `-`) or only numeric characters (`0-9`).

name: Create an issue
description: Creates a new issue in a GitHub repository, requiring the repository to exist and have issues enabled; specific fields like assignees, milestone, or labels may require push access.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The detailed textual contents of the new issue.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive). The repository must exist, be accessible, and have issues enabled.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub account owner of the repository (case-insensitive). The repository must exist and be accessible to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title for the new issue.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: Array of label names to associate with this issue (generally case-insensitive). NOTE: Only users with push access can set labels; they are silently dropped otherwise. Pass an empty list to clear all labels.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.description: Login for the user to whom this issue should be assigned. NOTE: Only users with push access can set the assignee; it is silently dropped otherwise. **This field is deprecated in favor of `assignees`.**

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.description: GitHub login names for users to assign to this issue. NOTE: Only users with push access can set assignees; they are silently dropped otherwise.

name: Create an organization webhook
description: Creates a webhook for a GitHub organization to deliver event notifications to a configured URL.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the webhook. Must be "web" for organization webhooks. Defaults to "web".

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Boolean indicating if the webhook is active. `true` sends notifications, `false` does not. Defaults to `true`.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: List of event names to trigger the webhook. Use `['*']` for all events. Defaults to `['push']` if not specified. See GitHub docs for event types.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.description: Required. The target URL for webhook payload delivery. This URL is a mandatory part of the webhook's `config` object and will receive POST requests for subscribed events.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.description: Optional secret for generating an HMAC hex digest for payload verification (X-Hub-Signature-256 header). If provided, this is used as the key.

inputs.name: config__password
inputs.description: Password for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it, corresponding to `config_username`.

inputs.name: config__username
inputs.description: Username for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it. Use only if your endpoint is protected by basic auth.

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.description: The media type for serializing webhook payloads. Supported: `json`, `form`. GitHub API defaults to `form` if this field is not set or is `None`.

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.description: Controls SSL certificate verification for `config_url`. '1' disables verification (not recommended), '0' enables. GitHub API defaults to '0' (verification enabled) if this field is not set or is `None`.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: config__url
requiredInputs.description: Required. The target URL for webhook payload delivery. This URL is a mandatory part of the webhook's `config` object and will receive POST requests for subscribed events.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: The name of the webhook. Must be "web" for organization webhooks. Defaults to "web".

optionalInputs.name: active
optionalInputs.description: Boolean indicating if the webhook is active. `true` sends notifications, `false` does not. Defaults to `true`.

optionalInputs.name: events
optionalInputs.description: List of event names to trigger the webhook. Use `['*']` for all events. Defaults to `['push']` if not specified. See GitHub docs for event types.

optionalInputs.name: config__secret

optionalInputs.name: config__password

optionalInputs.name: config__username
optionalInputs.description: Username for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it. Use only if your endpoint is protected by basic auth.

optionalInputs.name: config__content__type

name: Create a project card
description: Creates a project card in a GitHub project column (classic projects only). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) was sunset on August 23, 2024. This API only works with existing classic project columns. New classic projects cannot be created. Consider using GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead. The request body must contain either: - `note`: A text note for the card - OR `content_id` AND `content_type`: To associate an issue or pull request

inputs.name: note
inputs.description: The text content for the card. Use this to create a note-only card. Either 'note' OR both 'content_id' and 'content_type' must be provided.

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the project column where the card will be created. This value is used as a path parameter in the API request URL (e.g., `/projects/columns/{column_id}/cards`).

inputs.name: content_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the issue or pull request to associate with this card. Required when content_type is specified. Get this from the issue/PR details (it's the numeric ID, not the issue number).

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.description: Type of content to associate with the card.

name: Create a pull request
description: Creates a pull request in a GitHub repository, requiring existing `base` and `head` branches; `title` or `issue` must be provided.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: The name of the branch you want the changes pulled into. This must be an existing branch on the current (target) repository. You cannot submit a pull request to one repository that requests a merge to a base branch of another repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The detailed description or contents of the pull request.

inputs.name: head
inputs.description: The name of the branch where your changes are implemented. IMPORTANT: This branch must already exist in the repository (or fork) before calling this action. You must first create the branch and push commits to it (using git or the 'Create a reference' action), then create the PR. For cross-repository pull requests, namespace `head` with the source owner and branch, like `username:branch`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.description: Indicates whether the pull request should be created as a draft. Draft pull requests cannot be merged until marked as ready for review.

inputs.name: issue
inputs.description: The number of an existing issue in the repository to convert into a pull request. If provided, the issue's title and body may be used for the pull request. Required if `title` is not specified.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title of the new pull request. Required unless `issue` is specified.

inputs.name: head_repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World') where the changes in the pull request were made. This field is required for cross-repository pull requests if both the source and target repositories are owned by the same organization but are different repositories.

inputs.name: maintainer_can_modify
inputs.description: Indicates whether maintainers of the upstream repository can modify the pull request. This is primarily relevant for pull requests originating from forks.

requiredInputs.name: base
requiredInputs.description: The name of the branch you want the changes pulled into. This must be an existing branch on the current (target) repository. You cannot submit a pull request to one repository that requests a merge to a base branch of another repository.

requiredInputs.name: head
requiredInputs.description: The name of the branch where your changes are implemented. IMPORTANT: This branch must already exist in the repository (or fork) before calling this action. You must first create the branch and push commits to it (using git or the 'Create a reference' action), then create the PR. For cross-repository pull requests, namespace `head` with the source owner and branch, like `username:branch`.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

optionalInputs.name: body
optionalInputs.description: The detailed description or contents of the pull request.

optionalInputs.name: draft
optionalInputs.description: Indicates whether the pull request should be created as a draft. Draft pull requests cannot be merged until marked as ready for review.

optionalInputs.name: issue
optionalInputs.description: The number of an existing issue in the repository to convert into a pull request. If provided, the issue's title and body may be used for the pull request. Required if `title` is not specified.

optionalInputs.name: title
optionalInputs.description: The title of the new pull request. Required unless `issue` is specified.

optionalInputs.name: head_repo
optionalInputs.description: The name of the repository (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World') where the changes in the pull request were made. This field is required for cross-repository pull requests if both the source and target repositories are owned by the same organization but are different repositories.

name: Create a reference

name: Create a release
description: Creates a release in a GitHub repository for a specified tag; the tag must be unique for published releases, and if a `discussion_category_name` is given, it must already exist.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: A detailed description of the release. Markdown formatting is supported.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The title of the release. If omitted and `generate_release_notes` is false, it defaults to the `tag_name`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.description: Set to `true` to create an unpublished (draft) release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.description: The name of the tag for this release. This tag must be unique for published releases.

inputs.name: prerelease
inputs.description: Set to `true` to identify this release as a pre-release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: make_latest
inputs.description: Specifies if this release should be marked as the latest for the repository. Draft releases and prereleases cannot be set as 'latest'.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) from which the Git tag is created. Defaults to the repository's default branch if the tag specified in `tag_name` doesn't exist yet.

inputs.name: generate_release_notes
inputs.description: Set to `true` to automatically generate the release title (`name`) and description (`body`) from the commit history since the last release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: discussion_category_name
inputs.description: If specified, a new discussion will be created in this category and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository's discussions.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: tag_name
requiredInputs.description: The name of the tag for this release. This tag must be unique for published releases.

optionalInputs.name: body
optionalInputs.description: A detailed description of the release. Markdown formatting is supported.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: The title of the release. If omitted and `generate_release_notes` is false, it defaults to the `tag_name`.

optionalInputs.name: draft
optionalInputs.description: Set to `true` to create an unpublished (draft) release. Defaults to `false`.

optionalInputs.name: prerelease
optionalInputs.description: Set to `true` to identify this release as a pre-release. Defaults to `false`.

optionalInputs.name: make_latest
optionalInputs.description: Specifies if this release should be marked as the latest for the repository. Draft releases and prereleases cannot be set as 'latest'.

optionalInputs.name: target_commitish
optionalInputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) from which the Git tag is created. Defaults to the repository's default branch if the tag specified in `tag_name` doesn't exist yet.

optionalInputs.name: generate_release_notes
optionalInputs.description: Set to `true` to automatically generate the release title (`name`) and description (`body`) from the commit history since the last release. Defaults to `false`.

optionalInputs.name: discussion_category_name
optionalInputs.description: If specified, a new discussion will be created in this category and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository's discussions.

name: Create a repository ruleset
description: Creates a uniquely named ruleset for a repository, defining rules for branches or tags with specified enforcement, conditions, and bypass actors.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Unique name for the ruleset.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: rules
inputs.description: Array of rule objects as a JSON string to be enforced. Each must have `type` and may have `parameters`. Refer to GitHub API docs for available rule types and parameters. Example: '[{"type": "creation"}, {"type": "deletion"}]'

inputs.name: target
inputs.description: The target of the ruleset, whether it applies to branches or tags.

inputs.name: enforcement
inputs.description: Enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is not enforced), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (test rules before enforcing; GitHub Enterprise only).

inputs.name: bypass_actors
inputs.description: Actors that can bypass rules in this ruleset as a JSON string. Each actor object requires `actor_id`, `actor_type` (e.g., 'Team', 'Integration'), and `bypass_mode` (e.g., 'always'). Example: '[{"actor_id": 1, "actor_type": "Team", "bypass_mode": "always"}]'

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__exclude
inputs.description: Ref names or patterns to exclude. Condition fails if any pattern matches the ref name.

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__include
inputs.description: Ref names/patterns to include. Condition passes if one pattern matches the ref name. Use `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` for default branch, `~ALL` for all branches.

requiredInputs.description: Unique name for the ruleset.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

requiredInputs.description: Enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is not enforced), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (test rules before enforcing; GitHub Enterprise only).

name: Create a review comment for a pull request
description: Creates a review comment on a pull request's diff, targeting a specific line, range of lines, an entire file, or replying to an existing comment.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Text content of the review comment.

inputs.name: line
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE]** Line number in the diff for the comment. Required when using line-based mode (must be used with `side`). For multi-line comments, this is the last line of the range. Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Relative path of the file for the comment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: side
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE]** Side of the diff (`LEFT` or `RIGHT`) for the comment. Required when using line-based mode with the `line` parameter. For multi-line comments, refers to the last line of the range. Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters. **IMPORTANT:** Use `LEFT` only for deletions that appear in red in the diff. Use `RIGHT` for additions (green) or unchanged context lines (white). Attempting to comment on LEFT side lines that are not visible deletions in the diff will result in a 'could not be resolved' error.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: **[DEPRECATED - Use line-based mode instead]** Position in the diff hunk for the comment. When using position mode, do not provide line, side, start_line, start_side, or in_reply_to parameters.

inputs.name: commit_id
inputs.description: SHA of the commit for the comment. Using an outdated `commit_id` can misplace the comment if lines change.

inputs.name: start_line
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE - MULTI-LINE]** First line in the diff for a multi-line comment. Required when creating a multi-line comment (must be used with `start_side`, `line`, and `side`). Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters.

inputs.name: start_side
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE - MULTI-LINE]** Starting side of the diff (`LEFT` or `RIGHT`) for a multi-line comment. Required when creating a multi-line comment (must be used with `start_line`, `line`, and `side`). Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters. **IMPORTANT:** Use `LEFT` only for deletions that appear in red in the diff. Use `RIGHT` for additions (green) or unchanged context lines (white). Attempting to use LEFT for lines that are not visible deletions will result in a 'could not be resolved' error.

inputs.name: in_reply_to
inputs.description: **[REPLY MODE]** ID of an existing review comment to reply to. When using reply mode, do not provide position, line, side, start_line, or start_side parameters as they will be ignored.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: Identifier of the pull request.

requiredInputs.name: body
requiredInputs.description: Text content of the review comment.

requiredInputs.name: path
requiredInputs.description: Relative path of the file for the comment.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Repository name without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

requiredInputs.name: commit_id
requiredInputs.description: SHA of the commit for the comment. Using an outdated `commit_id` can misplace the comment if lines change.

requiredInputs.name: pull_number
requiredInputs.description: Identifier of the pull request.

optionalInputs.name: line
optionalInputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE]** Line number in the diff for the comment. Required when using line-based mode (must be used with `side`). For multi-line comments, this is the last line of the range. Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters.

optionalInputs.name: side
optionalInputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE]** Side of the diff (`LEFT` or `RIGHT`) for the comment. Required when using line-based mode with the `line` parameter. For multi-line comments, refers to the last line of the range. Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters. **IMPORTANT:** Use `LEFT` only for deletions that appear in red in the diff. Use `RIGHT` for additions (green) or unchanged context lines (white). Attempting to comment on LEFT side lines that are not visible deletions in the diff will result in a 'could not be resolved' error.

optionalInputs.name: position
optionalInputs.description: **[DEPRECATED - Use line-based mode instead]** Position in the diff hunk for the comment. When using position mode, do not provide line, side, start_line, start_side, or in_reply_to parameters.

optionalInputs.name: start_line
optionalInputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE - MULTI-LINE]** First line in the diff for a multi-line comment. Required when creating a multi-line comment (must be used with `start_side`, `line`, and `side`). Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters.

optionalInputs.name: start_side
optionalInputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE - MULTI-LINE]** Starting side of the diff (`LEFT` or `RIGHT`) for a multi-line comment. Required when creating a multi-line comment (must be used with `start_line`, `line`, and `side`). Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters. **IMPORTANT:** Use `LEFT` only for deletions that appear in red in the diff. Use `RIGHT` for additions (green) or unchanged context lines (white). Attempting to use LEFT for lines that are not visible deletions will result in a 'could not be resolved' error.

optionalInputs.name: in_reply_to
optionalInputs.description: **[REPLY MODE]** ID of an existing review comment to reply to. When using reply mode, do not provide position, line, side, start_line, or start_side parameters as they will be ignored.

name: Create a review for a pull request
description: Creates a pull request review, allowing approval, change requests, or comments; `body` is required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`, and omitting `event` makes the review `PENDING`.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Review's main body text; required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: Review action type (`APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, `COMMENT`); if omitted, review is `PENDING` and requires later submission.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: comments
inputs.description: Inline draft review comments. Each object requires `path` (relative file path) and `body` (comment text). Optionally, specify `line` (or `start_line` for multi-line), and `side` (or `start_side`) for diff location. `position` is deprecated; use `line`. Example: `[{'path': 'file.py', 'line': 10, 'body': 'Refactor this.'}]`

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The text of the comment

inputs.name: line
inputs.description: The line number in the diff to comment on (required for single-line comments)

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The relative path to the file being commented on

inputs.name: side
inputs.description: Which side of the diff to place the comment on. Use 'LEFT' for deleted lines (red in diff) or unchanged context on the old version. Use 'RIGHT' for added/changed lines (green in diff) or unchanged context on the new version. Defaults to 'RIGHT' if not specified.

inputs.name: start_line
inputs.description: The start line number for multi-line comments

inputs.name: start_side
inputs.description: Which side of the diff to start a multi-line comment on. Use 'LEFT' for deleted lines or unchanged context on the old version. Use 'RIGHT' for added/changed lines or unchanged context on the new version. Defaults to 'RIGHT' if not specified.

inputs.name: commit_id
inputs.description: SHA of the commit to review; defaults to the latest pull request commit. Outdated SHAs may lead to stale comments.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: Identifying number of the pull request.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

requiredInputs.name: pull_number
requiredInputs.description: Identifying number of the pull request.

optionalInputs.name: body
optionalInputs.description: Review's main body text; required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`.

optionalInputs.name: event
optionalInputs.description: Review action type (`APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, `COMMENT`); if omitted, review is `PENDING` and requires later submission.

optionalInputs.name: comments
optionalInputs.description: Inline draft review comments. Each object requires `path` (relative file path) and `body` (comment text). Optionally, specify `line` (or `start_line` for multi-line), and `side` (or `start_side`) for diff location. `position` is deprecated; use `line`. Example: `[{'path': 'file.py', 'line': 10, 'body': 'Refactor this.'}]`

optionalInputs.name: body
optionalInputs.description: The text of the comment

optionalInputs.name: line
optionalInputs.description: The line number in the diff to comment on (required for single-line comments)

optionalInputs.name: path
optionalInputs.description: The relative path to the file being commented on

optionalInputs.name: side
optionalInputs.description: Which side of the diff to place the comment on. Use 'LEFT' for deleted lines (red in diff) or unchanged context on the old version. Use 'RIGHT' for added/changed lines (green in diff) or unchanged context on the new version. Defaults to 'RIGHT' if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: start_line
optionalInputs.description: The start line number for multi-line comments

optionalInputs.name: start_side
optionalInputs.description: Which side of the diff to start a multi-line comment on. Use 'LEFT' for deleted lines or unchanged context on the old version. Use 'RIGHT' for added/changed lines or unchanged context on the new version. Defaults to 'RIGHT' if not specified.

optionalInputs.name: commit_id
optionalInputs.description: SHA of the commit to review; defaults to the latest pull request commit. Outdated SHAs may lead to stale comments.

name: Create a scoped access token
description: Exchanges a user-to-server token for a new, fine-grained scoped access token for a GitHub App, requiring `client_id`, `access_token`, either `target` or `target_id`, and at least one permission; for repository-specific scoping, provide either `repositories` (names) or `repository_ids` (IDs), but not both.

inputs.name: target
inputs.description: Username or organization name for scoping the new token. Required if `target_id` is not provided.

inputs.name: client_id
inputs.description: Client ID of the GitHub App.

inputs.name: target_id
inputs.description: ID of the user or organization for scoping the new token. Required if `target` is not provided.

inputs.name: access_token
inputs.description: User-to-server access token to be exchanged for a new scoped token.

inputs.name: repositories
inputs.description: Repository names to scope the new token to. Cannot be used if `repository_ids` is specified.

inputs.name: repository_ids
inputs.description: Repository IDs to scope the new token to. Cannot be used if `repositories` is specified.

inputs.name: permissions__pages
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Pages settings, statuses, configurations, builds, and creating new builds. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__checks
inputs.description: Permission for checks on code (e.g., CI statuses). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__issues
inputs.description: Permission for issues, comments, assignees, labels, and milestones. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__actions
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Actions workflows, workflow runs, and artifacts. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__members
inputs.description: Permission for organization members and teams. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__profile
inputs.description: Permission to manage profile settings for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__secrets
inputs.description: Permission for repository secrets (e.g., Actions secrets). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__contents
inputs.description: Permission for repository contents, commits, branches, downloads, releases, and merges. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__metadata
inputs.description: Read-only permission for repository metadata, search, and collaborator listing. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__packages
inputs.description: Permission for packages in GitHub Packages. Applies to target repositories or organization.

inputs.name: permissions__starring
inputs.description: Permission to list and manage repositories a user is starring. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__statuses
inputs.description: Permission for commit statuses. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__followers
inputs.description: Permission to manage followers for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__gpg__keys
inputs.description: Permission to view and manage GPG keys for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__workflows
inputs.description: Permission for updating GitHub Actions workflow files. Requires `contents: write`. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__codespaces
inputs.description: Permission for Codespaces. Applies to target user/organization if repositories are not specified, otherwise to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__deployments
inputs.description: Permission for deployments and deployment statuses. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__environments
inputs.description: Permission for repository environments. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__single__file
inputs.description: Permission to access specific files in targeted repositories. The file paths must be configured in the GitHub App settings.

inputs.name: permissions__administration
inputs.description: Permission for repository administration (settings, teams, collaborators). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__git__ssh__keys
inputs.description: Permission to manage Git SSH keys for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__pull__requests
inputs.description: Permission for pull requests, comments, assignees, labels, milestones, and merges. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__email__addresses
inputs.description: Permission to manage email addresses for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__security__events
inputs.description: Permission for security events (e.g., code scanning alerts). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__repository__hooks
inputs.description: Permission for repository webhooks. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__team__discussions
inputs.description: Permission for team discussions and related comments. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__plan
inputs.description: Read-only permission for viewing an organization's billing plan. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__dependabot__secrets
inputs.description: Permission for Dependabot secrets for targeted repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__interaction__limits
inputs.description: Permission to view and manage interaction limits on a repository. Applies to target repositories or organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__hooks
inputs.description: Permission for organization webhooks. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__events
inputs.description: Read-only permission to view organization audit log events. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__repository__projects
inputs.description: Permission for repository projects, columns, and cards. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__secrets
inputs.description: Permission for organization-level secrets (e.g., Actions secrets). Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__vulnerability__alerts
inputs.description: Permission for Dependabot alerts. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__packages
inputs.description: Permission for organization-level GitHub Packages. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__projects
inputs.description: Permission for organization projects and projects beta (if available). Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__secret__scanning__alerts
inputs.description: Permission for secret scanning alerts. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__custom__roles
inputs.description: Permission for custom repository roles within an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__administration
inputs.description: Permission for administrative settings of an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__user__blocking
inputs.description: Permission for viewing and managing users blocked by the organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__repository__custom__properties
inputs.description: Permission to view and edit repository custom properties, if allowed by property definition. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__custom__org__roles
inputs.description: Permission for custom organization roles. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__custom__properties
inputs.description: Permission for custom properties for an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__announcement__banners
inputs.description: Permission to view and manage organization announcement banners. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__self__hosted__runners
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Actions self-hosted runners for an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__personal__access__tokens
inputs.description: Permission for fine-grained personal access tokens requested by organization members. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__copilot__seat__management
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Copilot seat assignments for organization members (beta). Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__personal__access__token__requests
inputs.description: Permission for requests for fine-grained personal access tokens within an organization. Applies to target organization.

name: Create a dependency snapshot
description: Creates a snapshot of a repository's dependencies to populate the GitHub dependency graph and enable security alerts; `sha` must be a 40-character commit ID, `ref` a fully qualified Git reference (e.g., `refs/heads/main`), and `scanned` an ISO 8601 timestamp.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Git reference (fully qualified, e.g., `refs/heads/main`) of the repository branch/tag that triggered this snapshot.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: Commit SHA (maximum 40 characters) associated with this dependency snapshot.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without `.git` extension (name is not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (name is not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: job__id
inputs.description: Optional external ID of the job that generated this snapshot.

inputs.name: scanned
inputs.description: UTC timestamp (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) when the snapshot was taken.

inputs.name: version
inputs.description: Version of the dependency snapshot submission, typically of the manifest or lock file format.

inputs.name: metadata
inputs.description: Optional user-defined metadata as a JSON string for domain-specific information. Limited to 8 keys with scalar values (e.g., string, number, boolean). Example: '{"key1": "value1", "key2": 123}'

inputs.name: manifests
inputs.description: Optional collection of package manifests as a JSON string (e.g., package-lock.json, pom.xml). Keys are manifest file names; values detail dependencies. Example: '{"package-lock.json": {"name": "...", "resolved": {...}}}'

inputs.name: detector__url
inputs.description: Optional URL of the detector tool used.

inputs.name: detector__name
inputs.description: Optional name of the tool used to detect dependencies.

inputs.name: job__html__url
inputs.description: Optional URL for the job that generated this snapshot.

inputs.name: job__correlator
inputs.description: Optional string to group snapshots. For a given `job.correlator` and `detector.name`, only the 'latest' snapshot is current. Useful to distinguish detection runs (e.g., concatenating GITHUB_WORKFLOW and GITHUB_JOB).

inputs.name: detector__version
inputs.description: Optional version of the detector tool used.

name: Create a tag object
description: Creates an annotated Git tag object in a repository, pointing to an existing Git object (commit, tree, or blob) defined by its SHA and ensuring the `type` field correctly specifies the object's type.

inputs.name: tag
inputs.description: The name of the tag, typically a version identifier (e.g., "v0.0.1").

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of the Git object being tagged.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: object
inputs.description: The SHA of the Git object (commit, tree, or blob) that this tag is pointing to.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The message associated with the tag.

inputs.name: tagger__date
inputs.description: The timestamp for when this object was tagged. This should be in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: tagger__name
inputs.description: The name of the person creating the tag.

inputs.name: tagger__email
inputs.description: The email address of the person creating the tag.

requiredInputs.name: tag
requiredInputs.description: The name of the tag, typically a version identifier (e.g., "v0.0.1").

requiredInputs.name: repo
