name: Create a tree
description: Creates a new Git tree object in a repository, defining file/directory structure by specifying tree entries, optionally building on a `base_tree` SHA; all provided SHAs must be valid.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: tree
inputs.description: Array of objects defining the tree structure. Each object (node) requires `path` (string), `mode` (string; e.g., '100644' file, '100755' executable, '040000' subdirectory/tree, '160000' submodule, '120000' symlink), and `type` (string: 'blob', 'tree', or 'commit'). For new/updated blobs, provide `content` (UTF-8 string). For existing objects or `tree`/`commit` types, provide `sha` (string). To delete, specify `path`, `mode`, `type`, and set `sha` to `null`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: base_tree
inputs.description: SHA1 of an existing Git tree object to use as a base. If provided, its entries are combined with `tree` entries, where `tree` entries overwrite those from `base_tree` with the same path. Typically the SHA1 of the latest commit's tree for new branch changes. If omitted, the new tree is built from `tree` entries; files from the parent commit's tree not in `tree` are treated as deleted.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: tree
requiredInputs.description: Array of objects defining the tree structure. Each object (node) requires `path` (string), `mode` (string; e.g., '100644' file, '100755' executable, '040000' subdirectory/tree, '160000' submodule, '120000' symlink), and `type` (string: 'blob', 'tree', or 'commit'). For new/updated blobs, provide `content` (UTF-8 string). For existing objects or `tree`/`commit` types, provide `sha` (string). To delete, specify `path`, `mode`, `type`, and set `sha` to `null`.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

optionalInputs.name: base_tree
optionalInputs.description: SHA1 of an existing Git tree object to use as a base. If provided, its entries are combined with `tree` entries, where `tree` entries overwrite those from `base_tree` with the same path. Typically the SHA1 of the latest commit's tree for new branch changes. If omitted, the new tree is built from `tree` entries; files from the parent commit's tree not in `tree` are treated as deleted.

name: Create inference chat completions
description: Tool to create chat completions using GitHub Models inference API. Use when you need to generate AI-powered text responses using models like GPT-4, Claude, or Llama through GitHub's inference endpoint.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.description: If specified, the system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result.

inputs.name: stop
inputs.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

inputs.name: model
inputs.description: ID of the specific model to use for the request. Format: {publisher}/{model_name}, e.g., openai/gpt-4o, anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet.

inputs.name: tools
inputs.description: A list of tools (functions) the model may call. The model will choose when and how to call these functions based on the conversation context.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the function to be called.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of tool.

inputs.name: parameters
inputs.description: Schema defining function parameters.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of the parameters object, typically 'object'.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: List of required parameter names.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.description: Dictionary defining each parameter's schema.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A description of what the function does.

inputs.name: top_p
inputs.description: An alternative to temperature called nucleus sampling. Range: [0.0, 1.0]. The model considers tokens with top_p probability mass.

inputs.name: stream
inputs.description: If true, partial message deltas will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available.

inputs.name: messages
inputs.description: The collection of context messages associated with this chat completion request. Each message must have a role (assistant, developer, system, or user) and content.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: Role of the message sender.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The text content of the message.

inputs.name: max_tokens
inputs.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length.

inputs.name: modalities
inputs.description: List of supported modalities for this request. Supported values: text, audio.

inputs.name: temperature
inputs.description: Controls the apparent creativity of generated completions. Range: [0.0, 1.0]. Lower values make output more deterministic, higher values more random.

inputs.name: tool_choice
inputs.description: Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.

inputs.name: stream_options
inputs.description: Options for streaming responses.

inputs.name: include_usage
inputs.description: If true, usage information will be included in the streaming response.

inputs.name: response_format
inputs.description: Specifies the format of the model's output.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of response format.

inputs.name: presence_penalty
inputs.description: Influences probability of generated tokens appearing based on whether they appear in the text so far. Range: [-2.0, 2.0]. Positive values increase likelihood of new topics.

inputs.name: frequency_penalty
inputs.description: Influences probability of generated tokens appearing based on their cumulative frequency in the text so far. Range: [-2.0, 2.0]. Positive values decrease likelihood of repetition.

requiredInputs.name: model
requiredInputs.description: ID of the specific model to use for the request. Format: {publisher}/{model_name}, e.g., openai/gpt-4o, anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet.

requiredInputs.name: content
requiredInputs.description: The text content of the message.

inputs.description: The name of the secret. This name must be unique among the user's Codespaces secrets.

inputs.name: secret_value
inputs.description: The plaintext value for your secret. This will be automatically encrypted using the user's Codespaces public key before being sent to GitHub. If you already have an encrypted value, use the encrypted_value parameter instead.

inputs.description: Secret's value, encrypted using [LibSodium](https://libsodium.gitbook.io/doc/bindings_for_other_languages) with the public key (see `key_id`) obtained from GitHub's 'Get public key for the authenticated user' endpoint.

inputs.description: Repository IDs that can access this secret. If omitted or empty, accessible to all user's Codespaces-enabled repositories.

name: Create or update file contents
description: Creates a new file or updates an existing file in a GitHub repository; SHA can be provided to validate file updates, automatically fetched if not provided.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: The blob SHA of the file being replaced. Automatically fetched if not provided. If a 409 conflict occurs (SHA mismatch due to concurrent modifications), the action will automatically retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff and jitter.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The full path to the file in the repository, including the filename and extension. Do not include leading slashes.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository (not case sensitive, without the `.git` extension).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The branch name. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted. If a non-existent branch is specified, the action will automatically fall back to the repository's default branch. For empty repositories, omitting this parameter is recommended to let GitHub initialize with the repository's configured default branch.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The new file content. Can be provided as plain text (will be automatically encoded to Base64) or as Base64-encoded text. GitHub API requires Base64 encoding, which this action handles automatically.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The commit message for this file creation or update.

inputs.name: author__date
inputs.description: Author's timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to committer's date if author details are provided but date is not.

inputs.name: author__name
inputs.description: Author's name. If specified, `author_email` is also required. Defaults to committer details; if all committer/author details omitted, GitHub uses authenticated user.

inputs.name: author__email
inputs.description: Author's email. If specified, `author_name` is also required. Defaults to committer details; if all committer/author details omitted, GitHub uses authenticated user.

inputs.name: committer__date
inputs.description: Committer's commit timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to current time if committer details are provided but date is not.

inputs.name: committer__name
inputs.description: Committer's name. If specified, `committer_email` is also required. GitHub uses authenticated user if all committer/author details omitted.

inputs.name: committer__email
inputs.description: Committer's email. If specified, `committer_name` is also required. GitHub uses authenticated user if all committer/author details omitted.

name: Create or update repo secret with encrypted value
description: Creates or updates a Dependabot secret in a repository using an `encrypted_value` (pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the repository's Dependabot public key) and its corresponding `key_id`. This action allows you to securely manage secrets for a given repository, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected during the update process. By providing the encrypted value alongside the key ID, you can seamlessly maintain the necessary credentials for your repository's operations.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: The ID of the public key that was used to encrypt the secret value. This ID is retrieved from GitHub along with the public key via the 'Get a repository public key' endpoint for Dependabot secrets. If not provided or if an outdated key_id is used, the action will automatically fetch the latest public key from GitHub and retry with the current key_id.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the Dependabot secret to create or update.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value for your secret. This value must be encrypted with LibSodium, using the public key retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key' endpoint for Dependabot secrets. The resulting encrypted string is typically Base64 encoded.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: secret_name
requiredInputs.description: The name of the Dependabot secret to create or update.

optionalInputs.name: key_id
optionalInputs.description: The ID of the public key that was used to encrypt the secret value. This ID is retrieved from GitHub along with the public key via the 'Get a repository public key' endpoint for Dependabot secrets. If not provided or if an outdated key_id is used, the action will automatically fetch the latest public key from GitHub and retry with the current key_id.

optionalInputs.name: encrypted_value
optionalInputs.description: Value for your secret. This value must be encrypted with LibSodium, using the public key retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key' endpoint for Dependabot secrets. The resulting encrypted string is typically Base64 encoded.

name: Create Sponsors Tier
description: Tool to create a new payment tier for your GitHub Sponsors profile using GraphQL. Use when you need to add a new sponsorship tier with a specific monthly price and description. The amount is specified in dollars and will be converted to cents automatically.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: The monthly sponsorship amount in dollars (USD). This value will be converted to cents internally (e.g., 5 for $5/month becomes 500 cents, 500 for $500/month becomes 50000 cents). Minimum is $1.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A detailed description of what this sponsorship tier provides. Explain the benefits and perks sponsors will receive at this level. This is a required field.

inputs.name: sponsorable_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the user or organization who will receive sponsorships for this tier (e.g., 'U_kgDOABCDEF' for users, 'O_kgDOGHIJKL' for organizations). Use the get_viewer_graphql_id action to fetch your own ID.

inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations and track request origin.

name: Create or Update Organization Secret with LibSodium
description: Creates or updates a Dependabot organization secret, requiring the secret value to be pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the organization's public key obtained from the 'Get an organization public key' endpoint.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: ID of the Dependabot public key used for encryption (retrieved from 'Get an organization public key for Dependabot' endpoint: GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/secrets/public-key). Required if `encrypted_value` is provided.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Secret visibility: `all` (all repositories), `private` (private repositories), or `selected` (specific repositories designated by `selected_repository_ids`).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The secret name (case-insensitive and unique within the organization).

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value of the secret, encrypted using [LibSodium](https://libsodium.gitbook.io/doc/bindings_for_other_languages) and the organization's Dependabot public key (obtained from the 'Get an organization public key for Dependabot' endpoint: GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/secrets/public-key). Required to create or update the secret's value.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: List of repository IDs that can access the secret; required and used only when `visibility` is `selected`.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The organization name (case-insensitive).

requiredInputs.name: visibility
requiredInputs.description: Secret visibility: `all` (all repositories), `private` (private repositories), or `selected` (specific repositories designated by `selected_repository_ids`).

requiredInputs.name: secret_name
requiredInputs.description: The secret name (case-insensitive and unique within the organization).

optionalInputs.name: key_id
optionalInputs.description: ID of the Dependabot public key used for encryption (retrieved from 'Get an organization public key for Dependabot' endpoint: GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/secrets/public-key). Required if `encrypted_value` is provided.

optionalInputs.name: encrypted_value
optionalInputs.description: Value of the secret, encrypted using [LibSodium](https://libsodium.gitbook.io/doc/bindings_for_other_languages) and the organization's Dependabot public key (obtained from the 'Get an organization public key for Dependabot' endpoint: GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/secrets/public-key). Required to create or update the secret's value.

optionalInputs.name: selected_repository_ids

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: The type of package to delete. Supported types: 'npm' (Node.js packages), 'maven' (Java/Gradle packages), 'rubygems' (Ruby gems), 'docker' (legacy Docker images from docker.pkg.github.com), 'nuget' (.NET packages), 'container' (Docker images from ghcr.io). Note: GitHub Gradle packages use 'maven' type; ghcr.io images use 'container' type.

name: Find Pull Requests
description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Filter by specific repository. Can be provided in 'owner/repo' format (e.g., 'facebook/react'), or as just the repo name when used with the owner parameter. When owner and repo are both provided and have the same value, they are combined as 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with owner, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The repository must exist and be accessible with your token. Prefer 'owner/repo' format; bare repo name without the `owner` parameter causes invalid repository format errors.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Field to sort results by. Set explicitly to 'updated' with `order=desc` when recency matters; default behavior may return best-match relevance ordering instead.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: Filter by label name.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order (ascending or descending).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Filter by repository owner (user or organization). Can be used alone to search all repositories for an owner, or combined with repo parameter to specify owner and repo separately. When both owner and repo are provided, the search is scoped to 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with repo, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The owner must exist and repositories must be accessible with your token.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Optional search query for PR title, description, or commit messages. Supports GitHub search syntax including label filters (e.g., 'label:bug'), logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), and text search. If not provided, PRs will be listed based on other filter parameters. Type qualifiers (type:pr, is:pr, is:issue, type:issue) are automatically normalized. When using logical operators with qualifiers like labels, the query is automatically grouped for proper parsing. When omitting query, ensure at least one structured filter parameter is provided; passing no query and no filters may cause validation errors. Prefer dedicated parameters (`label`, `state`, `author`) over embedding qualifiers in the query string. Exact title matching is not supported; post-filter on title client-side if needed.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: Filter by PR state: 'open', 'closed', or 'all'.

inputs.name: author
inputs.description: Filter by PR author username. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.description: Filter by PR assignee username. Use '@me' for current user. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Filter by programming language.

inputs.name: mentions
inputs.description: Filter by username mentioned in PR. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total search results at ~1000 items; iterate `page` until results fewer than `per_page` are returned, and add `repo`, `label`, or date filters when totals approach 1000.

inputs.name: is_merged
inputs.description: Filter by merge status. True for merged PRs, False for unmerged, None for all.

inputs.name: base_branch
inputs.description: Filter by base branch name.

inputs.name: head_branch
inputs.description: Filter by head branch name.

inputs.name: created_since
inputs.description: Filter PRs created after this date (ISO 8601 format). Provide as UTC ISO 8601 timestamp to avoid off-by-one-day errors at range boundaries. Does not filter on `merged_at`.

inputs.name: updated_since
inputs.description: Filter PRs updated after this date (ISO 8601 format).

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.description: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

inputs.name: for_authenticated_user
inputs.description: Include private repositories accessible to the authenticated user in search results. When True without repo/owner, searches PRs involving you (author, assignee, mentions, commenter) across all accessible repos. When False without repo/owner, restricts to public repos only. When repo/owner is specified, this flag has no effect on scope. Set to True when searching private repositories; False searches all of GitHub and produces large noisy result sets.

optionalInputs.name: page
optionalInputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

optionalInputs.name: repo

When owner and repo are both provided and have the same value, they are combined as 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with owner, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The repository must exist and be accessible with your token. Prefer 'owner/repo' format; bare repo name without the `owner` parameter causes invalid repository format errors.

optionalInputs.name: sort

The owner must exist and repositories must be accessible with your token.

name: Find Repositories
description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Field to sort results by.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order (ascending or descending).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Filter by specific user or organization. Do NOT use this parameter if your 'query' already contains 'user:', 'org:', or 'repo:' qualifiers - they will conflict. Use either 'owner' parameter OR search qualifiers in 'query', not both.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Search query for repository name, description, or README content. Accepts both 'query' and 'q' as parameter names. Supports OR operator for alternatives (e.g., 'bug OR error'). Multi-word phrases in OR expressions are automatically quoted. GitHub limits OR expressions to 6 terms maximum; queries exceeding this limit are truncated. If using GitHub qualifiers like 'repo:', 'user:', or 'org:', they must be properly formatted (e.g., 'repo:owner/name', 'user:username', 'org:orgname'). Note: 'owner:' is not a valid GitHub qualifier; if used, it will be automatically transformed to 'user:'. Must be non-empty; blank strings trigger a 'Search query cannot be empty' validation error.

inputs.name: topic
inputs.description: Filter by repository topic/tag.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.description: Filter by archived status. None means include both.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Filter by programming language.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total results at ~1000 per query; for exhaustive searches, split by `language`, `topic`, `min_stars`/`max_stars`, or `owner`.

inputs.name: max_stars
inputs.description: Maximum number of stars the repository should have.

inputs.name: min_forks
inputs.description: Minimum number of forks the repository should have.

inputs.name: min_stars
inputs.description: Minimum number of stars the repository should have.

inputs.name: fork_filter
inputs.description: How to handle forks: 'include' (include forks), 'exclude' (exclude forks), 'only' (only forks).

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.description: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

inputs.name: for_authenticated_user
inputs.description: Search only in repositories accessible to the authenticated user (including private repos). Requires token scopes `repo`/`read:org`; sparse results compared to GitHub UI typically indicate insufficient token scopes.

requiredInputs.name: query
requiredInputs.description: Search query for repository name, description, or README content. Accepts both 'query' and 'q' as parameter names. Supports OR operator for alternatives (e.g., 'bug OR error'). Multi-word phrases in OR expressions are automatically quoted. GitHub limits OR expressions to 6 terms maximum; queries exceeding this limit are truncated. If using GitHub qualifiers like 'repo:', 'user:', or 'org:', they must be properly formatted (e.g., 'repo:owner/name', 'user:username', 'org:orgname'). Note: 'owner:' is not a valid GitHub qualifier; if used, it will be automatically transformed to 'user:'. Must be non-empty; blank strings trigger a 'Search query cannot be empty' validation error.

name: Generate release notes content for a release
description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.description: The tag name for which the release notes are to be generated. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., a branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) that will be the target for the release's tag. This is required if the `tag_name` does not reference an existing tag. It is ignored if `tag_name` already exists.

inputs.name: previous_tag_name
inputs.description: The name of the previous tag to use as the starting point for the release notes. This manually specifies the range of changes to be included. If omitted, GitHub will attempt to automatically determine the previous release.

inputs.name: configuration_file_path
inputs.description: Specifies a path to a configuration file in the repository (e.g., `.github/release.yml`) containing settings for generating release notes. If unspecified, GitHub looks for a configuration file at ".github/release.yml" or ".github/release.yaml". If no such file is found, default settings are used.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: tag_name
requiredInputs.description: The tag name for which the release notes are to be generated. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

optionalInputs.name: target_commitish
optionalInputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., a branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) that will be the target for the release's tag. This is required if the `tag_name` does not reference an existing tag. It is ignored if `tag_name` already exists.

optionalInputs.name: previous_tag_name
optionalInputs.description: The name of the previous tag to use as the starting point for the release notes. This manually specifies the range of changes to be included. If omitted, GitHub will attempt to automatically determine the previous release.

optionalInputs.name: configuration_file_path
optionalInputs.description: Specifies a path to a configuration file in the repository (e.g., `.github/release.yml`) containing settings for generating release notes. If unspecified, GitHub looks for a configuration file at ".github/release.yml" or ".github/release.yaml". If no such file is found, default settings are used.

inputs.description: Number of results per page when paginating the commit's diff (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: Get a reference
description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The Git reference to retrieve. Must be formatted as `heads/<branch_name>` for branches, `tags/<tag_name>` for tags, or `pull/<number>/head` (or `pull/<number>/merge`) for pull requests. Simple branch names (e.g., `main`) are automatically converted to `heads/<name>`. The reference must exist in the repository; a 404 error is returned if not found. If unsure which references exist, use 'list_matching_references' or 'get_a_branch' first. Note: Commit SHAs are not supported by this endpoint; use commit-specific endpoints instead. Not all repositories use 'master'; many now use 'main' as the default branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: ref

name: List repository files and directories
description: Browse and list all files/directories in a GitHub repository. Efficiently retrieves the complete repository structure in a single API call when used with recursive mode. Perfect for analyzing codebase structure, finding specific files, or getting an overview of repository contents without cloning.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name without owner or .git extension (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, repo is 'kubernetes').

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Repository owner username or organization name (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, owner is 'kubernetes').

inputs.name: tree_sha
inputs.description: Branch name (e.g., 'main', 'master'), tag name (e.g., 'v1.0.0'), commit SHA, or tree SHA. Directory paths are NOT supported - use a branch/tag to get the full tree, then extract the directory's SHA from results.

inputs.name: recursive
inputs.description: Set to True (or '1'/'true') to fetch the ENTIRE repository file tree recursively (all files and directories in one call). Set to False or leave empty to fetch only the top-level files/folders. For browsing or analyzing the full repository structure, use recursive=True.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Repository name without owner or .git extension (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, repo is 'kubernetes').

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Repository owner username or organization name (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, owner is 'kubernetes').

requiredInputs.name: tree_sha
requiredInputs.description: Branch name (e.g., 'main', 'master'), tag name (e.g., 'v1.0.0'), commit SHA, or tree SHA. Directory paths are NOT supported - use a branch/tag to get the full tree, then extract the directory's SHA from results.

optionalInputs.name: recursive

name: Get Raw Repository Content
description: Tool to fetch raw file content from a GitHub repository. Returns raw bytes in whatever format the file uses (JSON, CSV, binary, etc.) — parsing and validation are the caller's responsibility. Ensure owner, repo, ref, and path are consistent when combining with other GitHub tools to avoid mismatched-ref errors.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The branch name, tag name, or commit SHA to fetch the file from. Defaults to the repository's default branch (usually 'main' or 'master'). Recommended to pass explicitly — omitting it fetches from the default branch, which may not match the ref used in other tool calls and can yield unexpected or missing content.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The file path within the repository, relative to the repository root. Use forward slashes for directory separators (e.g., 'src/utils/helper.js'). Do not include leading slash. Do NOT pass a full GitHub URL - only the relative path within the repo. Must exactly match the file path as it exists in the repository — use GITHUB_GET_A_TREE to discover valid paths rather than guessing directory structure.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The repository name (not the full URL). This is the repository name shown in the repository URL (e.g., for github.com/torvalds/linux, the repo is 'linux'). Do not include '.git' suffix. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This is the account name shown in the repository URL (e.g., for github.com/torvalds/linux, the owner is 'torvalds'). Case-insensitive.

requiredInputs.name: path
requiredInputs.description: The file path within the repository, relative to the repository root. Use forward slashes for directory separators (e.g., 'src/utils/helper.js'). Do not include leading slash. Do NOT pass a full GitHub URL - only the relative path within the repo. Must exactly match the file path as it exists in the repository — use GITHUB_GET_A_TREE to discover valid paths rather than guessing directory structure.

requiredInputs.name: repo

name: Get repository content
description: Retrieves a file's Base64 encoded content or lists a directory's contents from a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The name of the commit, branch, or tag to read from. Use this parameter to specify which branch (e.g., 'feat/godlocal-dev', 'main', 'develop'), tag (e.g., 'v1.2.0'), or commit SHA you want to access. If not provided, the repository's default branch will be used. Note: Branch names should be passed here, NOT in the path parameter.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Required. Path to file or directory in the repository. Empty string ('') retrieves root directory contents. Leading slashes auto-stripped. CRITICAL: Do NOT pass branch names here (e.g., 'feat/godlocal-dev', 'main'). Branch names belong in the `ref` parameter. Path should be file/directory path only (e.g., 'src/main.js', 'README.md'), NOT prefixed with branch name (use 'src/main.js' not 'main/src/main.js'). File paths must include extension (e.g., 'README.md', not 'README'). For common files (README, LICENSE, CHANGELOG) without extensions, action auto-tries common extensions (.md, .txt, .rst). Paths are case-sensitive. On 404 errors, list parent directory to verify exact path/casing.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Required. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Required. The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive. Can also accept full repository path in format 'owner/repo' - the repo part will be extracted automatically.

requiredInputs.name: path

Paths are case-sensitive. On 404 errors, list parent directory to verify exact path/casing.

name: List commits
description: Lists commits for a GitHub repository. Requires 'owner' (username/org) and 'repo' (repository name) parameters. Optionally filter by SHA/branch, path, author, committer, or date range.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: SHA hash or branch name to start listing commits from. If not provided, the GitHub API uses the repository's default branch (usually `main` or `master`).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of the results to fetch when using pagination.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Only commits modifying this specific file path will be returned.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension (repository name only, not the full 'owner/repo' path). Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens (-), underscores (_), and periods (.). Wildcards like '*' are not supported.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name only, not the full 'owner/repo' path). Must contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens, cannot start/end with a hyphen, max 39 characters. Wildcards are not supported.

inputs.name: since
inputs.description: Only commits created on or after this timestamp will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: until
inputs.description: Only commits created before this timestamp will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: author
inputs.description: Filter commits by the commit author's GitHub login or email address.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

inputs.name: committer
inputs.description: Filter commits by the commit committer's GitHub login or email address. The committer is the user who applied the patch and may differ from the original author.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension (repository name only, not the full 'owner/repo' path). Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens (-), underscores (_), and periods (.). Wildcards like '*' are not supported.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name only, not the full 'owner/repo' path). Must contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens, cannot start/end with a hyphen, max 39 characters. Wildcards are not supported.

optionalInputs.name: sha
optionalInputs.description: SHA hash or branch name to start listing commits from. If not provided, the GitHub API uses the repository's default branch (usually `main` or `master`).

optionalInputs.name: page
optionalInputs.description: Page number of the results to fetch when using pagination.

optionalInputs.name: path
optionalInputs.description: Only commits modifying this specific file path will be returned.

optionalInputs.name: since
optionalInputs.description: Only commits created on or after this timestamp will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

optionalInputs.name: until
optionalInputs.description: Only commits created before this timestamp will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

optionalInputs.name: author
optionalInputs.description: Filter commits by the commit author's GitHub login or email address.

optionalInputs.name: per_page
optionalInputs.description: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

optionalInputs.name: committer
optionalInputs.description: Filter commits by the commit committer's GitHub login or email address. The committer is the user who applied the patch and may differ from the original author.

name: List global security advisories
description: Lists GitHub's global security advisories, filterable by various attributes including ID, type, ecosystem, severity, and dates.

inputs.name: cwes
inputs.description: Filter by Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifiers. Accepts comma-separated string or multiple parameters. Example: `cwes=79,284`.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Property to sort results by (updated, published, epss_percentage, epss_percentile).

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Filter by advisory type (e.g., reviewed, malware). Defaults to 'reviewed'; if this default is active and no other search parameters are used, malware advisories are also excluded.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: Pagination cursor to retrieve results after this value.

inputs.name: before
inputs.description: Pagination cursor to retrieve results before this value.

inputs.name: cve_id
inputs.description: Filter by Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) identifier.

inputs.name: affects
inputs.description: Filter by affected 'package' or 'package@version'. Max 100 items. Example: `affects=pkg1,pkg2@1.0.0`.

inputs.name: ghsa_id
inputs.description: Filter by GitHub Security Advisory (GHSA) identifier.

inputs.name: updated
inputs.description: Filter by update date or date range. For syntax, see GitHub's 'understanding the search syntax' documentation.

inputs.name: modified
inputs.description: Filter by last modification (update or publication) date or date range. For syntax, see GitHub's 'understanding the search syntax' documentation.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: severity
inputs.description: Filter by advisory severity (e.g., high, critical).

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: Sort direction for results (asc, desc).

inputs.name: ecosystem
inputs.description: Filter by package ecosystem (e.g., npm, pip).

inputs.name: published
inputs.description: Filter by publication date or date range. For syntax, see GitHub's 'understanding the search syntax' documentation.

inputs.name: is_withdrawn
inputs.description: Filter for withdrawn advisories.

inputs.name: epss_percentage
inputs.description: Filter by EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) percentage score. Use comparison operators (e.g., '>0.5', '<=0.1').

inputs.name: epss_percentile
inputs.description: Filter by EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) percentile score. Use comparison operators (e.g., '>0.9', '>=0.5').

optionalInputs.name: cwes
optionalInputs.description: Filter by Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifiers. Accepts comma-separated string or multiple parameters. Example: `cwes=79,284`.

name: List issue comments for a repository
description: Lists issue comments, including those on pull requests, for an accessible repository.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: The page number of the results to fetch, used for pagination. Default is 1. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is a path parameter needed to construct the API URL. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Specifies the field by which to sort the issue comments. Valid values are `created` (for creation date, default) or `updated` (for last update date).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This is a path parameter needed to construct the API URL. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: since
inputs.description: Filters comments to include only those last updated at or after the specified time. This should be a timestamp in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of issue comments to retrieve per page. Default is 30, maximum is 100. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: The direction for sorting results. Valid values are `asc` (ascending) or `desc` (descending). This parameter is effective only when `sort` is also specified.

name: List notifications for authenticated user
description: Tool to list notification threads for the authenticated user with efficient polling support. Use when you need to fetch the user's GitHub notification inbox with filters like unread/participating/since. Returns notification threads plus polling headers (Last-Modified, ETag, X-Poll-Interval) for efficient polling. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a Personal Access Token (classic) with 'notifications' or 'repo' scope. It does NOT work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens. If the token type is incompatible, a clear error message will be returned.

name: List org resource access tokens
description: Lists approved fine-grained personal access tokens (PATs) with access to resources in a GitHub organization, optionally filtering by owner, repository, permissions, or last usage time. AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (read). Standard personal access tokens CANNOT use this endpoint. The GitHub App must also have access to the organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the GitHub organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch (starts from 1).

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Property to sort results by. Currently only 'created_at' is supported.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Filter by a list of owner usernames (max 10 items).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: token_id
inputs.description: Filter by a list of token IDs (max 50 items). Use this to look up specific tokens.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending or 'desc' for descending (default).

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Filter by permission string in format 'scope:level' where scope is the permission area (e.g., 'issues', 'actions', 'contents') and level is 'read' or 'write'. Common examples: 'issues:read', 'issues:write', 'actions:read', 'actions:write', 'contents:read', 'contents:write', 'metadata:read', 'pull_requests:write'.

inputs.name: repository
inputs.description: Filter by repository name.

inputs.name: last_used_after
inputs.description: Filter for tokens last used after this ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

inputs.name: last_used_before
inputs.description: Filter for tokens last used before this ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

name: List pending team invitations
description: Lists all pending membership invitations for a specified team within an organization. The authenticated user must be an organization member with the read:org scope. The response includes invitation details such as the invitee's login/email, the role they're being invited to, and who sent the invitation. The 'role' field indicates the type of invitation: - 'direct_member': Regular organization member - 'admin': Organization administrator - 'billing_manager': Billing manager role - 'hiring_manager': Hiring manager role - 'reinstate': Reinstating a previous member Note: If the invitee is not a GitHub member, the 'login' field will be null and only the 'email' field will be populated.

inputs.name: org

name: List project cards (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Lists all project cards for a given column_id in GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns a list of project cards including: id, node_id, url, note (or null for linked cards), creator info, content_url (for issue/PR cards), column_url, project_url, timestamps, and archived status.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to retrieve (1-indexed). Default is 1.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of project cards to return per page (1-100). Default is 30.

inputs.name: column_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the project column for which to list cards. This ID can be obtained from the 'list_project_columns' action or from the column's API URL (e.g., /projects/columns/12345).

inputs.name: archived_state
inputs.description: Filters the project cards based on their archived state. Options: "all" returns all cards, "archived" returns only archived cards, and "not_archived" returns only unarchived/active cards.

name: List project collaborators (classic)
description: Lists collaborators for a GitHub Project (classic) by project ID. IMPORTANT: This action uses the Projects (classic) REST API which was sunset on April 1, 2025 for GitHub.com. The API may still be available on GitHub Enterprise Server versions before 3.17. For GitHub.com users: GitHub Projects (classic) has been replaced by the new Projects experience. The new Projects does not have a REST API for listing collaborators - use the GitHub web interface to manage project collaborators instead. You must be an organization owner or a project admin to list collaborators.

name: List pull requests
description: Lists pull requests for a GitHub repository. REQUIRES 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters - both are MANDATORY and must be provided in every call. Does NOT support filtering by assignee or date range - use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead for searching PRs by assignee, author, or across multiple repositories.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: Filter by base branch name.

inputs.name: head
inputs.description: Filter by head user/org and branch name, e.g., `user:ref-name`.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number for results.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: [REQUIRED - MUST BE PROVIDED] The name of the repository (without the `.git` extension). This parameter is mandatory and cannot be omitted. Example: 'Spoon-Knife' or 'vscode'. To search PRs across multiple repos, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Sorts results. Valid values: 'created', 'updated', 'popularity', 'long-running'. 'popularity' is by comment count; 'long-running' by age and recent activity.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: [REQUIRED - MUST BE PROVIDED] The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This parameter is mandatory and cannot be omitted. Example: 'octocat' or 'microsoft'. To search PRs across all repos without specifying owner, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: Filter by pull request state. Valid values: 'open', 'closed', 'all'.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: Sort direction. Valid values: 'asc', 'desc'. Defaults to 'desc' if sort is 'created' or unspecified, else 'asc'.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: [REQUIRED - MUST BE PROVIDED] The name of the repository (without the `.git` extension). This parameter is mandatory and cannot be omitted. Example: 'Spoon-Knife' or 'vscode'. To search PRs across multiple repos, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: [REQUIRED - MUST BE PROVIDED] The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This parameter is mandatory and cannot be omitted. Example: 'octocat' or 'microsoft'. To search PRs across all repos without specifying owner, use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead.

optionalInputs.name: base
optionalInputs.description: Filter by base branch name.

optionalInputs.name: head
optionalInputs.description: Filter by head user/org and branch name, e.g., `user:ref-name`.

optionalInputs.name: page
optionalInputs.description: Page number for results.

optionalInputs.name: sort
optionalInputs.description: Sorts results. Valid values: 'created', 'updated', 'popularity', 'long-running'. 'popularity' is by comment count; 'long-running' by age and recent activity.

optionalInputs.name: state
optionalInputs.description: Filter by pull request state. Valid values: 'open', 'closed', 'all'.

optionalInputs.name: per_page
optionalInputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

optionalInputs.name: direction
optionalInputs.description: Sort direction. Valid values: 'asc', 'desc'. Defaults to 'desc' if sort is 'created' or unspecified, else 'asc'.

name: List repository activities
description: Lists activities for a GitHub repository, ensuring the repository exists and is accessible.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The Git reference for which to list activities (e.g., a branch name like `main` or a full ref like `refs/heads/main`). If unspecified, activities from all branches are listed.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: actor
inputs.description: The GitHub username of the person who performed the activity. Filters activities to only those performed by this user.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: A pagination cursor. Returns activity items that occurred after this cursor. This cursor is typically obtained from the `Link` header in a previous API response. For more details, see GitHub's REST API documentation on "Using pagination in the REST API" and "Using Link headers".

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: before
inputs.description: A pagination cursor. Returns activity items that occurred before this cursor. This cursor is typically obtained from the `Link` header in a previous API response. For more details, see GitHub's REST API documentation on "Using pagination in the REST API" and "Using Link headers".

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of activity results to return per page. Defaults to 30, maximum is 100. For more information, see the GitHub REST API guide on "Using pagination in the REST API".

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: The direction to sort the activity results. Use 'asc' for ascending (oldest first) or 'desc' for descending (newest first).

inputs.name: time_period
inputs.description: Filters activities based on a predefined time period. For example, 'day' retrieves activities from the last 24 hours, and 'week' from the last 7 days (168 hours).

inputs.name: activity_type
inputs.description: Filters activities by a specific type, such as 'push' or 'pr_merge'. If unspecified, all relevant activity types are returned.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

optionalInputs.name: ref
optionalInputs.description: The Git reference for which to list activities (e.g., a branch name like `main` or a full ref like `refs/heads/main`). If unspecified, activities from all branches are listed.

name: List repository issues
description: Lists issues (including pull requests) from a GitHub repository. Requires BOTH 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters (e.g., owner='octocat', repo='Hello-World' for github.com/octocat/Hello-World).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number for the set of results. Refer to GitHub's REST API documentation for pagination details.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The name of the repository (without .git extension). For 'octocat/Hello-World', repo is 'Hello-World'. Not case-sensitive. Must be provided together with 'owner'.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: The field to sort the results by.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Filter issues by type name. Use '*' for issues with any type, 'none' for issues without a type, or specify a type name.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The GitHub account owner (user or organization) of the repository. For 'octocat/Hello-World', owner is 'octocat'. Not case-sensitive. Must be provided together with 'repo'.

inputs.name: since
inputs.description: Filters issues to include only those updated at or after the specified time. This should be a timestamp in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: Filters issues based on their state.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of label names to filter issues by. Example: 'bug,ui,@high'.

inputs.name: creator
inputs.description: Filter issues by the username of the user who created the issue.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.description: Filter issues by assignee's username. Use 'none' for issues with no assignee, or '*' for issues assigned to any user.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100). Default is 30, which matches GitHub's API default. Refer to GitHub's REST API documentation for pagination details.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: The direction of the sort.

inputs.name: mentioned
inputs.description: Filter issues by a user mentioned in the issue's description or comments. Provide the username.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.description: Filter issues by milestone. Provide the milestone `number` as a string, '*' for issues with any milestone, or 'none' for issues without a milestone.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: REQUIRED. The name of the repository (without .git extension). For 'octocat/Hello-World', repo is 'Hello-World'. Not case-sensitive. Must be provided together with 'owner'.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: REQUIRED. The GitHub account owner (user or organization) of the repository. For 'octocat/Hello-World', owner is 'octocat'. Not case-sensitive. Must be provided together with 'repo'.

optionalInputs.name: page
optionalInputs.description: Page number for the set of results. Refer to GitHub's REST API documentation for pagination details.

optionalInputs.name: sort
optionalInputs.description: The field to sort the results by.

optionalInputs.name: type
optionalInputs.description: Filter issues by type name. Use '*' for issues with any type, 'none' for issues without a type, or specify a type name.

optionalInputs.name: since
optionalInputs.description: Filters issues to include only those updated at or after the specified time. This should be a timestamp in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

optionalInputs.name: state
optionalInputs.description: Filters issues based on their state.

optionalInputs.name: labels
optionalInputs.description: A comma-separated list of label names to filter issues by. Example: 'bug,ui,@high'.

optionalInputs.name: creator
optionalInputs.description: Filter issues by the username of the user who created the issue.

optionalInputs.name: assignee
optionalInputs.description: Filter issues by assignee's username. Use 'none' for issues with no assignee, or '*' for issues assigned to any user.

optionalInputs.name: per_page
optionalInputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100). Default is 30, which matches GitHub's API default. Refer to GitHub's REST API documentation for pagination details.

optionalInputs.name: direction
optionalInputs.description: The direction of the sort.

optionalInputs.name: mentioned
optionalInputs.description: Filter issues by a user mentioned in the issue's description or comments. Provide the username.

optionalInputs.name: milestone
optionalInputs.description: Filter issues by milestone. Provide the milestone `number` as a string, '*' for issues with any milestone, or 'none' for issues without a milestone.

name: Manage access control for organization codespaces
description: Sets the Codespaces access control policy for a GitHub organization, determining which members can use them. This endpoint requires admin permissions on the organization. The API returns HTTP 204 No Content on success.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). The authenticated user must have admin permissions on this organization.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description:
Defines which users can access Codespaces within the organization.
`disabled`: Codespaces are disabled for all users in the organization.
`selected_members`: Only users specified in `selected_usernames` can access Codespaces.
`all_members`: All members of the organization can access Codespaces.
`all_members_and_outside_collaborators`: All members and outside collaborators can access Codespaces.

inputs.name: selected_usernames
inputs.description: A list of GitHub usernames who should have access to Codespaces. Required when `visibility` is set to `selected_members`. Providing a list here will replace any existing list of selected usernames. The specified users must be members or outside collaborators of the organization.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). The authenticated user must have admin permissions on this organization.

requiredInputs.name: visibility
requiredInputs.description:
Defines which users can access Codespaces within the organization.
`disabled`: Codespaces are disabled for all users in the organization.
`selected_members`: Only users specified in `selected_usernames` can access Codespaces.
`all_members`: All members of the organization can access Codespaces.
`all_members_and_outside_collaborators`: All members and outside collaborators can access Codespaces.

optionalInputs.name: selected_usernames
optionalInputs.description: A list of GitHub usernames who should have access to Codespaces. Required when `visibility` is set to `selected_members`. Providing a list here will replace any existing list of selected usernames. The specified users must be members or outside collaborators of the organization.

name: Merge a pull request
description: Merges an open and mergeable pull request in a repository. A 405 error can occur for multiple reasons: (1) The PR is still in draft mode - convert it to ready-for-review first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST. (2) Branch protection rules are not satisfied - check for required approving reviews, status checks, or other repository rules that must be met before merging. (3) The base branch has been modified since the PR was last synced - update the PR branch first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST_BRANCH.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: SHA of the pull request's head commit. Must match for merge to succeed, ensuring PR hasn't changed since review.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Repository owner's username. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the pull request. The PR must be mergeable: not a draft, and all branch protection rules (required reviews, status checks, etc.) must be satisfied.

inputs.name: commit_title
inputs.description: Title for the merge commit message.

inputs.name: merge_method
inputs.description: Merge strategy: 'merge', 'squash', or 'rebase'. Defaults to repository's setting if unspecified.

inputs.name: commit_message
inputs.description: Additional details for the merge commit message.

requiredInputs.name: repo

requiredInputs.name: owner

name: Move a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Moves a project card to a specified position within a column (GitHub Classic Projects). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. It may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects remain enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ On success, the API returns HTTP 201 with an empty body, indicating the card was moved.

inputs.name: card_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the project card to be moved. Obtain this ID from the list_project_cards or get_a_project_card actions.

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: The target position of the card within the column. name: Move a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
description: Moves a column within a GitHub project (classic) to a new position. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. The `position` parameter can be: - 'first': Move the column to the beginning of the project - 'last': Move the column to the end of the project - 'after:<column_id>': Place the column after the specified column (e.g., 'after:67890') See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: The new position for the column within the project. Valid values are: 'first' (move to the beginning), 'last' (move to the end), or 'after:<column_id>' (place after the specified column, e.g., 'after:67890').

inputs.description: The unique identifier of the project column to be moved. Must be a valid column ID from a GitHub project (classic).

requiredInputs.name: position
requiredInputs.description: The new position for the column within the project. Valid values are: 'first' (move to the beginning), 'last' (move to the end), or 'after:<column_id>' (place after the specified column, e.g., 'after:67890').

requiredInputs.name: column_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique identifier of the project column to be moved. Must be a valid column ID from a GitHub project (classic).

name: Privately report a security vulnerability
description: Privately reports a security vulnerability to a repository's maintainers. IMPORTANT: You cannot report vulnerabilities to repositories you own - this API is for third-party security researchers to report to other repositories. The target repository must have private vulnerability reporting enabled. Specify EITHER `severity` OR `cvss_vector_string`, but not both.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: cwe_ids
inputs.description: A list of Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifiers relevant to the vulnerability.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.description: A short, descriptive summary of the security vulnerability.

inputs.name: severity
inputs.description: The advisory's severity level. Mutually exclusive with `cvss_vector_string`.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A detailed explanation of the security vulnerability, its impact, and potential mitigations.

inputs.name: vulnerabilities
inputs.description: JSON string representing a list of vulnerability objects detailing the affected packages or products. Each object should specify the package's ecosystem and name, the vulnerable version range, and optionally, patched versions and vulnerable functions.

inputs.name: cvss_vector_string
inputs.description: The CVSS vector string for advisory severity. Mutually exclusive with `severity`.

inputs.name: start_private_fork
inputs.description: Whether to create a temporary private fork to collaborate on a fix for the reported vulnerability.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: summary
requiredInputs.description: A short, descriptive summary of the security vulnerability.

requiredInputs.name: description
requiredInputs.description: A detailed explanation of the security vulnerability, its impact, and potential mitigations.

optionalInputs.name: cwe_ids
optionalInputs.description: A list of Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifiers relevant to the vulnerability.

optionalInputs.name: severity
optionalInputs.description: The advisory's severity level. Mutually exclusive with `cvss_vector_string`.

optionalInputs.name: vulnerabilities

optionalInputs.name: cvss_vector_string
optionalInputs.description: The CVSS vector string for advisory severity. Mutually exclusive with `severity`.

optionalInputs.name: start_private_fork
optionalInputs.description: Whether to create a temporary private fork to collaborate on a fix for the reported vulnerability.

name: Remove status check contexts
description: Removes specified status check contexts from a protected branch's required status checks. This action removes the specified status check contexts from the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GraphQL API documentation.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: The status check contexts to remove from the protected branch. These are the names of CI/CD jobs or other checks that should no longer be required before merging. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings. Examples: 'ci/test', 'linter', 'continuous-integration/travis-ci'.

name: Remove team access restrictions
description: Removes specified teams from push access to a protected branch. This action removes teams from the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. Removing a team will also remove push access for any child teams. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must currently have push access to the branch

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization name that owns the repository. Team access restrictions only work for organization-owned repositories, not personal repositories. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: teams
inputs.description: The list of team slugs to remove from push access to the protected branch. A team slug is the URL-friendly version of the team name (lowercase, hyphens instead of spaces). Removing a team will also remove push access for any child teams. This removes teams from the existing list (does not replace the entire list).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the protected branch to remove team restrictions from. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner

Removing a team will also remove push access for any child teams. This removes teams from the existing list (does not replace the entire list).

requiredInputs.name: branch
requiredInputs.description: The name of the protected branch to remove team restrictions from. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

It will fail if: - Team synchronization with an Identity Provider (IdP) is enabled (403 Forbidden) - The organization, team, or user membership doesn't exist (404 Not Found) - You don't have sufficient permissions to manage team memberships (403 Forbidden) Note: If the removed user was the last member and the team is not nested, the team may be deleted automatically.

name: Replace repository access for an org Codespaces secret
description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access an organization-level Codespaces secret. This operation completely replaces the existing list of repositories with the provided list. The secret must already have its visibility set to 'selected' for this operation to succeed. Use this when you want to set an exact list of repositories that should have access. Prerequisites: - The organization must exist and you must have admin:org scope - The Codespaces secret must already exist in the organization - The secret's visibility must be set to 'selected' (not 'all' or 'private') Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success, 404 if secret/org not found, 409 if visibility is not 'selected'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The GitHub organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: Name of the development environment secret for which repository access is being configured.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: Array of repository IDs that can access the organization Codespaces secret. This completely replaces any existing list of repositories. Note: The secret's visibility must already be set to 'selected' (not 'all' or 'private') for this operation to succeed; otherwise, a 409 Conflict error is returned.

requiredInputs.name: org

name: Review Custom Deployment Rules For Workflow Run
description: Approves or rejects pending custom deployment protection rules for a workflow run. This endpoint allows GitHub Apps to review their own pending custom deployment protection rules for a specific workflow run. The review can either approve (allowing the deployment to proceed) or reject (blocking the deployment). **Important notes:** - GitHub Apps can only review their own custom deployment protection rules - This endpoint requires GitHub App installation access tokens with Deployments permission (write) - Custom deployment protection rules are available in public repos for all plans; for private/internal repos, GitHub Enterprise is required - Returns 204 No Content on success

inputs.description: Repository name, without `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: state

inputs.description: Unique identifier of the workflow run.

inputs.name: comment
inputs.description: Optional comment to include with the review decision.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the deployment environment to review (e.g., 'production', 'staging').

name: Review PAT Requests in Bulk
description: Approves or denies multiple fine-grained personal access token requests for an organization in bulk. This endpoint processes multiple PAT request approvals or denials in a single API call. All specified pat_request_ids must refer to currently pending requests. Important: This endpoint can ONLY be called by GitHub Apps with the 'organization_personal_access_token_requests:write' permission. It cannot be called with personal access tokens or OAuth apps. On success, returns HTTP 202 Accepted with an empty body.

inputs.name: action

inputs.name: reason
inputs.description: Optional reason for the decision. Maximum 1024 characters. This reason is stored and may be visible to the requester.

inputs.name: pat_request_ids
inputs.description: List of unique identifiers for the fine-grained personal access token requests to review. Must contain between 1 and 100 request IDs. Get these IDs from the 'List requests to access organization resources' endpoint.

name: Revoke an installation access token
description: Revokes the GitHub App's current installation access token, immediately invalidating it for API authentication. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token (ghs_* prefix) for authentication. OAuth tokens (gho_*) or personal access tokens are not supported and will result in a 403 Forbidden error. After successful revocation, the token becomes permanently invalid and cannot be used for any subsequent API calls. To continue API operations, a new installation access token must be generated.

name: Run GraphQL Query
description: Tool to run an arbitrary GitHub GraphQL v4 query or mutation. Use when fetching multiple datasets in one batch. Cost-based rate limit: ~5,000 points/hour; keep field selections narrow, avoid deep nesting, and include rateLimit in responses to monitor quota. Responses are nested under data.data (e.g., data.data.repository.vulnerabilityAlerts); always inspect the errors array even on HTTP 200, as partial failures embed there. Paginate by looping on pageInfo.hasNextPage and advancing with pageInfo.endCursor. Search results cap at ~1,000 items per query; split by label, date range, or repository for full coverage. Use ProjectV2 nodes, not Classic Projects (deprecated). Parallel mutations (e.g., addProjectV2ItemById) can trigger transient conflicts; use sequential execution.

inputs.description: GraphQL query or mutation string. Must comply with GitHub's GraphQL v4 schema.

name: Search code (all pages)
description: Tool to search code across multiple pages using GitHub's code search API. Use when single-page searches may miss matches and you need a full or capped result set. GitHub caps results at ~1,000 total items regardless of pagination; results for broad queries may be silently truncated. Only the default branch is indexed, and very large files may be excluded — treat results as potentially partial. Rate limit: ~30 requests/minute; honor `Retry-After` on 403/429 responses.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: The search query string for code search. **IMPORTANT**: While GitHub's documentation mentions boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT) support, the REST API still uses older syntax that frequently rejects queries with operators or parentheses (ERROR_TYPE_QUERY_PARSING_FATAL). For reliable searches, avoid boolean operators and parentheses. Supports qualifiers: `language:`, `repo:`, `path:`, `extension:`, `user:`, `org:`, `in:file`. Multiple terms use implicit AND: `hello world` finds both. Quote exact phrases: `"exact phrase"`. For complex boolean logic, use multiple separate API calls or the GitHub web interface.
