inputs.name: accept
inputs.description: Custom Accept header for media type (e.g., `application/vnd.github.text-match+json` to include text match metadata).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (min 1, max 100).

name: Search commits
description: Finds commits on GitHub using a query string (q) supporting keywords and qualifiers, with options for sorting and pagination.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Search query with at least one text keyword plus optional qualifiers. GitHub requires actual search text (commit message keywords) - qualifiers alone are not allowed. Common qualifiers: 'repo:owner/name', 'author:username', 'committer:username', 'author-date:YYYY-MM-DD' or 'author-date:>YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD', 'committer-date:YYYY-MM-DD', 'merge:true/false', 'hash:HASH', 'parent:HASH', 'tree:HASH'. IMPORTANT: The 'hash:' qualifier requires a full 40-character hexadecimal commit hash (e.g., 'hash:e5bd3914e2e596debea16f433f57875b5b90bcd6'). Do NOT use branch names, tag names, or short refs with 'hash:' - use 'repo:owner/name' with other filters to search commits on a specific branch.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to retrieve.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Sorts results by 'author-date' or 'committer-date'. Use separately with the 'order' parameter (e.g., sort='committer-date' with order='desc'). Do not combine them into a single value. If unspecified, GitHub defaults to 'best match' ranking.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order: 'desc' (newest first) or 'asc' (oldest first). Use with the 'sort' parameter to control direction (e.g., sort='committer-date' with order='desc').

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

This avoids the need to list commits per repository and prevents 409 errors on empty repos.

Examples: 'fix author:octocat', 'bug repo:owner/repo', 'update author:octocat author-date:>=2025-01-01'. If only qualifiers are provided, a wildcard search term will be automatically added.

name: Search issues and pull requests
description: Searches GitHub for issues and pull requests. Supports keywords, qualifiers (repo:, org:, user:, state:, label:, author:, assignee:, mentions:, etc.), and type filters (is:issue, is:pr). Type filters cannot be used alone - they must be combined with at least one keyword or other qualifier. All other qualifiers work independently. The @me shorthand (e.g., assignee:@me, mentions:@me) is automatically resolved to your username. Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) are supported but limited to a maximum of 5 operators total per query.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Search query using GitHub's search syntax. Supports keywords, qualifiers (repo:, org:, user:, state:, label:, author:, assignee:, mentions:, etc.), and type filters (is:issue, is:pr). IMPORTANT: Type filters (is:issue, is:pr) cannot be used alone - they must be combined with at least one keyword or other qualifier. All other qualifiers work independently (e.g., 'state:open', 'label:bug', 'assignee:octocat'). The @me shorthand (e.g., assignee:@me, mentions:@me, author:@me) can be used to reference the authenticated user. Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) can be used to combine terms (maximum 5 operators total).

name: Search repositories
description: Searches GitHub repositories using a flexible query (keywords, qualifiers) with sorting, ordering, and pagination. IMPORTANT: The 'q' parameter (search query) is REQUIRED and must always be provided.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: REQUIRED non-empty search query. Max 5 AND/OR/NOT operators. Qualifiers: `language:LANG`, `stars:N`, `stars:>N`, `forks:N`, `size:N` (KB), `topic:TOPIC`, `license:LICENSE`, `created:DATE`, `pushed:DATE`, `is:public|private`, `archived:true|false`, `in:name|description|readme`, `user:USERNAME`, `org:ORGNAME`. Must use real GitHub usernames/orgs (e.g., `user:octocat`), not placeholders. Queries with logical operators must include at least one plain keyword; `topic:solana OR topic:ethereum` fails, use `solana OR ethereum stars:>100` instead.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: The page number for pagination of search results. Defaults to 1. Used in conjunction with `per_page` to fetch subsequent sets of results. See GitHub API documentation for more on pagination.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Specifies the field to sort the search results by. Options include `stars`, `forks`, `help-wanted-issues`, `updated` (recency of update), or `created` (creation date). If not provided, results are sorted by GitHub's default 'best match' ranking.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Determines the order of search results: `desc` for descending or `asc` for ascending. This parameter is only effective when `sort` is also specified. Defaults to `desc`.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of repository results to return per page. The maximum value is 100. Defaults to 30. See GitHub API documentation for more on pagination.

requiredInputs.name: q
requiredInputs.description: REQUIRED non-empty search query. Max 5 AND/OR/NOT operators. Qualifiers: `language:LANG`, `stars:N`, `stars:>N`, `forks:N`, `size:N` (KB), `topic:TOPIC`, `license:LICENSE`, `created:DATE`, `pushed:DATE`, `is:public|private`, `archived:true|false`, `in:name|description|readme`, `user:USERNAME`, `org:ORGNAME`. Must use real GitHub usernames/orgs (e.g., `user:octocat`), not placeholders. Queries with logical operators must include at least one plain keyword; `topic:solana OR topic:ethereum` fails, use `solana OR ethereum stars:>100` instead.

optionalInputs.name: page
optionalInputs.description: The page number for pagination of search results. Defaults to 1. Used in conjunction with `per_page` to fetch subsequent sets of results. See GitHub API documentation for more on pagination.

optionalInputs.name: sort
optionalInputs.description: Specifies the field to sort the search results by. Options include `stars`, `forks`, `help-wanted-issues`, `updated` (recency of update), or `created` (creation date). If not provided, results are sorted by GitHub's default 'best match' ranking.

optionalInputs.name: order
optionalInputs.description: Determines the order of search results: `desc` for descending or `asc` for ascending. This parameter is only effective when `sort` is also specified. Defaults to `desc`.

optionalInputs.name: per_page
optionalInputs.description: The number of repository results to return per page. The maximum value is 100. Defaults to 30. See GitHub API documentation for more on pagination.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Set app access restrictions
description: Replaces the list of GitHub Apps permitted to push to a protected branch. This action completely replaces the existing list of apps with push access - it does not add to or merge with the current list. Requirements: - The branch must already have protection rules enabled with push restrictions configured - Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permission - Push restrictions are only available for organization-owned repositories with GitHub Team or Enterprise Cloud plans

inputs.name: apps
inputs.description: The list of GitHub App slugs to grant push access. This replaces the entire list of apps with push access. Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permission. The app slug is the URL-friendly name of the GitHub App (e.g., 'github-actions', 'dependabot').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization or username that owns the repository. Push restrictions are only available for organization-owned repositories.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the protected branch. Wildcards are not permitted. The branch must already have protection rules enabled with push restrictions configured.

name: Set team access restrictions
description: Replaces the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action sets (replaces) the teams that have push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Add team access restrictions' (POST), this action replaces the entire list rather than adding to it. Use an empty list to remove all team restrictions. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization name that owns the repository. Team access restrictions only work for organization-owned repositories, not personal repositories. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: teams
inputs.description: The list of team slugs to grant push access to the protected branch. A team slug is the URL-friendly version of the team name (lowercase, hyphens instead of spaces). The teams must already have access to the repository. This REPLACES the entire existing list of teams with push access (use empty list to remove all teams).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the protected branch to set team restrictions on. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

The teams must already have access to the repository. This REPLACES the entire existing list of teams with push access (use empty list to remove all teams).

requiredInputs.name: branch

name: Set user access restrictions
description: Replaces the list of users with push access to a protected branch in an organization repository. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled - This REPLACES (not adds to) the existing list of users with push access - Users must have write access to the repository to be added - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items - To add users without replacing, use the 'add_user_access_restrictions' action instead

inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (must be an organization for user restrictions). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: users
inputs.description: The list of user logins (usernames) to grant push access to the protected branch. This REPLACES the existing list of users. Users must have write access to the repository. Pass an empty list [] to remove all user restrictions. The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcards in branch names, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: Set workflow access level outside repository
description: Sets the access level for workflows outside a repository to use actions and reusable workflows within that repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Owner's username or organization name. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: access_level
inputs.description:
Specifies the level of access workflows outside this repository have to its actions and reusable workflows.
- `none`: Actions and reusable workflows are accessible only from workflows within this repository.
- `user`: Actions and reusable workflows can be shared with other private repositories owned by the same user.
- `organization`: Actions and reusable workflows can be shared with other repositories within the same organization.

- `user`: Actions and reusable workflows can be shared with other private repositories owned by the same user.
- `organization`: Actions and reusable workflows can be shared with other repositories within the same organization.

name: Stop a codespace for an organization user
description: Stops a running codespace for an organization member. This action allows organization administrators to stop a codespace that belongs to a member of their organization. The codespace must be in a running state (e.g., 'Available') to be stopped. Required permissions: - OAuth/PAT: admin:org scope - Fine-grained tokens: 'Organization codespaces' (write) or 'Codespaces lifecycle admin' (write) Common HTTP responses: - 200: Codespace successfully stopped, returns codespace object - 304: Not modified (codespace already stopped) - 401: Authentication required - 403: Insufficient permissions (need admin:org) - 404: Organization, user, or codespace not found

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (login). Case-insensitive. The authenticated user must have admin:org permissions for this organization.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username (login) of the organization member whose codespace will be stopped.

inputs.description: The unique name of the codespace to stop. This is typically a hyphenated string like 'username-repo-name-randomchars'. You can obtain this from the 'List codespaces for a user in organization' action.

name: Unlock an organization repository
description: Unlocks a repository that was locked for migration within a GitHub organization. This action is part of the organization migrations workflow. When you start a migration with `lock_repositories=true`, the source repositories are locked to prevent changes during export. After the migration is complete and applied to the target instance, use this action to unlock the repositories so they can be accessed or deleted. Requirements: - You must be an organization owner to unlock repositories - The repository must be currently locked as part of the specified migration - This endpoint does not work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens Typical workflow: 1. Start an organization migration with POST /orgs/{org}/migrations 2. Wait for the migration to complete (state: "exported") 3. Apply the migration to your target instance 4. Unlock each migrated repository using this action 5. Delete the source repositories if no longer needed

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization that owns the migration. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: repo_name
inputs.description: The name of the repository to unlock. Must be one of the repositories included in the specified migration.

inputs.name: migration_id
inputs.description: The unique numeric identifier of the organization migration. You can find this by listing organization migrations using GET /orgs/{org}/migrations.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization that owns the migration. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: repo_name

name: Update a check run
description: Updates an existing check run for a specific commit in a repository, allowing modifications to its status, conclusion, output, and other details. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot update check runs.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the check (e.g., "code-coverage").

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

inputs.name: actions
inputs.description: JSON string representing user-triggerable actions (max 3), each with `label`, `identifier`, and `description`.

inputs.name: conclusion
inputs.description: Final conclusion of the check. Required if `completed_at` is provided or `status` is `completed`. Providing `conclusion` sets `status` to `completed`. Users cannot set `stale`.

inputs.name: started_at
inputs.description: Timestamp of when the check run started (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: details_url
inputs.description: URL of the integrator's site with full check details.

inputs.name: external_id
inputs.description: Reference for the run on the integrator's system.

inputs.name: check_run_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the check run.

inputs.name: completed_at
inputs.description: Timestamp of when the check run completed (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: output__text
inputs.description: Text for the output, can contain Markdown.

inputs.name: output__title
inputs.description: Title for the check run output. Displayed prominently in the GitHub checks UI.

inputs.name: output__images
inputs.description: JSON string representing images for the output, displayed in the GitHub PR UI.

inputs.name: output__summary
inputs.description: Summary of the output, can contain Markdown. Required if the `output` object is provided.

inputs.name: output__annotations
inputs.description: JSON string representing line-specific analysis information, visible in PRs. Max 50 per request (appended to existing). GitHub Actions: 10 warnings/10 errors per step.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Repository name without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

requiredInputs.name: check_run_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique identifier of the check run.

optionalInputs.name: name

For example, to match branches that begin with `release/` and contain an additional single slash, use `release/*/*`. For more information about pattern matching syntax, refer to the Ruby File.fnmatch documentation (https://ruby-doc.org/core-2.5.1/File.html#method-c-fnmatch).

name: Update an organization variable
description: Updates an existing GitHub Actions organization variable's name, value, or visibility (`all`, `private`, `selected`), requiring `selected_repository_ids` with valid repository IDs if visibility is `selected`.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The current name of the organization variable to be updated. This name is case-sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Optional: The new value for the organization variable. If provided, the variable's value will be updated. If omitted or null, the current value is preserved.

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.description: Optional: The new name for the organization variable. If provided, the variable will be renamed. If omitted or null, the current name is preserved.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Optional: Specifies the new visibility of the organization variable. Determines which repositories can access this variable: 'all' (all repositories), 'private' (only private repositories), or 'selected' (specific repositories). When set to 'selected', you must also provide `selected_repository_ids`. If omitted or null, visibility remains unchanged.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: Optional: A list of repository IDs (integers) that can access this organization variable. Required when `visibility` is set to 'selected'. Ignored for other visibility values. If omitted when visibility='selected', the API may reject the request or use existing repository access list.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: name
requiredInputs.description: The current name of the organization variable to be updated. This name is case-sensitive.

optionalInputs.name: value
optionalInputs.description: Optional: The new value for the organization variable. If provided, the variable's value will be updated. If omitted or null, the current value is preserved.

optionalInputs.name: new_name
optionalInputs.description: Optional: The new name for the organization variable. If provided, the variable will be renamed. If omitted or null, the current name is preserved.

optionalInputs.name: visibility
optionalInputs.description: Optional: Specifies the new visibility of the organization variable. When set to 'selected', you must also provide `selected_repository_ids`. If omitted or null, visibility remains unchanged.

optionalInputs.name: selected_repository_ids
optionalInputs.description: Optional: A list of repository IDs (integers) that can access this organization variable. Required when `visibility` is set to 'selected'. Ignored for other visibility values. If omitted when visibility='selected', the API may reject the request or use existing repository access list.

name: Update an organization webhook
description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification), subscribed events, active status, or name of an existing webhook for a specified organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Must be passed as 'web' for organization webhooks. This is a fixed value required by the GitHub API.

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Set to `true` to send notifications, or `false` to disable them.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: List of events that trigger this webhook. GitHub REPLACES the full event list when this is sent — omit (leave unset) to preserve the existing event subscription. Use `['*']` to subscribe to all events. See GitHub docs for available event types.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the webhook. This ID can be found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.description: Target URL for webhook payloads. Required if updating other `config` properties (e.g., `content_type`, `secret`), as GitHub mandates `url` when `config` is modified.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.description: Secret key for HMAC digest to ensure payload integrity. Refer to GitHub documentation on delivery headers for details.

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.description: Media type for serializing payloads (`json` or `form`). The API defaults to `form` if this field is not provided.

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.description: Controls SSL certificate verification for payload delivery. Set to '1' to skip verification (insecure), or '0' to enforce (API default). Use with caution.

requiredInputs.description: The name of the organization. This value is not case-sensitive.

name: Update a project
description: Updates an existing GitHub project (V2) attributes like title, description, visibility, and state. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed. At least one field to update must be provided.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization that owns the project. If provided, this takes precedence over username.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title for the project. If not provided, the title will not be changed.

inputs.name: closed
inputs.description: Sets whether the project is closed. `True` closes the project, `False` reopens it. If not provided, the state will not be changed.

inputs.name: public
inputs.description: Sets the visibility of the project. `True` makes it public (visible to everyone), `False` makes it private. If not provided, the visibility will not be changed.

inputs.name: readme
inputs.description: The project's README content (supports Markdown formatting). If not provided, the README will not be changed.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username who owns the project. If not provided, defaults to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's sequential number (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This is displayed in the project URL as https://github.com/users/{username}/projects/{project_number}.

inputs.name: short_description
inputs.description: A short description for the project (displayed in project cards and listings). If not provided, the description will not be changed.

requiredInputs.name: project_number
requiredInputs.description: The project's sequential number (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This is displayed in the project URL as https://github.com/users/{username}/projects/{project_number}.

optionalInputs.name: org
optionalInputs.description: The organization that owns the project. If provided, this takes precedence over username.

optionalInputs.name: title
optionalInputs.description: The new title for the project. If not provided, the title will not be changed.

optionalInputs.name: closed
optionalInputs.description: Sets whether the project is closed. `True` closes the project, `False` reopens it. If not provided, the state will not be changed.

optionalInputs.name: public
optionalInputs.description: Sets the visibility of the project. `True` makes it public (visible to everyone), `False` makes it private. If not provided, the visibility will not be changed.

optionalInputs.name: readme
optionalInputs.description: The project's README content (supports Markdown formatting). If not provided, the README will not be changed.

optionalInputs.name: username
optionalInputs.description: The GitHub username who owns the project. If not provided, defaults to the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.name: short_description
optionalInputs.description: A short description for the project (displayed in project cards and listings). If not provided, the description will not be changed.

name: Update a pull request
description: Updates an existing pull request, allowing changes to attributes like title, body, state, base branch, and maintainer modification settings.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: The name of the branch to which you want your changes pulled. This must be an existing branch in the current repository. Cannot update the base branch to point to another repository. If None, the base branch remains unchanged.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The new contents of the pull request. To remove the body, pass an empty string. If None, the body remains unchanged.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: The new state of the pull request. Can be 'open' or 'closed'. If None, the state remains unchanged.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title for the pull request. If None, the title remains unchanged.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number that identifies the pull request within the repository.

inputs.name: maintainer_can_modify
inputs.description: Indicates whether [maintainers can modify](https://docs.github.com/articles/allowing-changes-to-a-pull-request-branch-created-from-a-fork/) the pull request. If None, this setting remains unchanged.

requiredInputs.name: repo

requiredInputs.name: owner

requiredInputs.name: pull_number
requiredInputs.description: The unique number that identifies the pull request within the repository.

optionalInputs.name: base

description: Updates an open pull request's head branch by merging the latest changes from its base branch, if mergeable and repository merging is enabled; operates asynchronously.

name: Update a reference
description: Updates an existing Git reference (e.g., a branch or tag) to point to a specific commit SHA. By default, only allows fast-forward updates (where the new commit is a descendant of the current one). Set `force=true` to allow non-fast-forward updates that rewrite history. IMPORTANT: The reference must already exist - use 'Create a reference' action to create new refs.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The name of the Git reference to update WITHOUT the 'refs/' prefix. For branches: use 'heads/<branch-name>' (e.g., 'heads/main', 'heads/feature-branch'). For tags: use 'tags/<tag-name>' (e.g., 'tags/v1.0.0'). Note: Branch references require 'heads/' (plural), not 'head/' (singular). Bare branch names will be automatically prefixed with 'heads/'. The reference must already exist - this endpoint only updates existing refs.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: The SHA1 commit hash to point this reference to. Must be exactly 40 hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f) representing a valid commit that exists in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, excluding `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: force
inputs.description: Controls whether to allow non-fast-forward updates. When `false` (default), the new commit must be a direct descendant of the current commit (fast-forward only) - this prevents accidental history rewriting. When `true`, allows moving the reference to any commit, even if it means rewriting history or moving backward. Set to `true` only when you need to force-push changes, reset a branch, or intentionally rewrite commit history.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

requiredInputs.name: ref
requiredInputs.description: The name of the Git reference to update WITHOUT the 'refs/' prefix. For branches: use 'heads/<branch-name>' (e.g., 'heads/main', 'heads/feature-branch'). For tags: use 'tags/<tag-name>' (e.g., 'tags/v1.0.0'). Note: Branch references require 'heads/' (plural), not 'head/' (singular). Bare branch names will be automatically prefixed with 'heads/'. The reference must already exist - this endpoint only updates existing refs.

requiredInputs.name: sha
requiredInputs.description: The SHA1 commit hash to point this reference to. Must be exactly 40 hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f) representing a valid commit that exists in the repository.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: Repository name, excluding `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

optionalInputs.name: force
optionalInputs.description: Controls whether to allow non-fast-forward updates. When `false` (default), the new commit must be a direct descendant of the current commit (fast-forward only) - this prevents accidental history rewriting. When `true`, allows moving the reference to any commit, even if it means rewriting history or moving backward. Set to `true` only when you need to force-push changes, reset a branch, or intentionally rewrite commit history.

name: Update a release
description: Updates an existing release in a GitHub repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion_category_name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Text describing the contents of the release. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The title of the release.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.description: Set to `true` to mark the release as a draft, or `false` to publish it. Default: `false`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.description: The name of the tag. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

inputs.name: prerelease
inputs.description: Set to `true` to identify the release as a pre-release, or `false` for a full release. Default: `false`.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the release to be updated.

inputs.name: make_latest
inputs.description: Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the repository. Accepts 'true', 'false', or 'legacy'. Drafts and pre-releases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true` for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name or commit SHA) that determines where the Git tag is created from. Can be any branch or commit SHA. This parameter is unused if the Git tag specified by `tag_name` already exists. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted.

inputs.name: discussion_category_name
inputs.description: If specified, a discussion of this category is created and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository. This parameter is ignored if a discussion is already linked to the release. For more information, see "[Managing categories for discussions in your repository](https://docs.github.com/discussions/managing-discussions-for-your-community/managing-categories-for-discussions-in-your-repository)."

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: release_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique identifier of the release to be updated.

optionalInputs.name: body
optionalInputs.description: Text describing the contents of the release. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: The title of the release.

optionalInputs.name: draft
optionalInputs.description: Set to `true` to mark the release as a draft, or `false` to publish it. Default: `false`.

optionalInputs.name: tag_name
optionalInputs.description: The name of the tag. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

optionalInputs.name: prerelease
optionalInputs.description: Set to `true` to identify the release as a pre-release, or `false` for a full release. Default: `false`.

optionalInputs.name: make_latest
optionalInputs.description: Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the repository. Accepts 'true', 'false', or 'legacy'. Drafts and pre-releases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true` for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.

optionalInputs.name: target_commitish
optionalInputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name or commit SHA) that determines where the Git tag is created from. Can be any branch or commit SHA. This parameter is unused if the Git tag specified by `tag_name` already exists. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted.

optionalInputs.name: discussion_category_name
optionalInputs.description: If specified, a discussion of this category is created and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository. This parameter is ignored if a discussion is already linked to the release. For more information, see "[Managing categories for discussions in your repository](https://docs.github.com/discussions/managing-discussions-for-your-community/managing-categories-for-discussions-in-your-repository)."

name: Update a repository
description: Updates settings for an existing GitHub repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: New name for the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: ACTUAL name of an existing repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real repository that exists and is accessible - placeholder values like 'your-repo-name' will cause 404 errors.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: ACTUAL GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real, existing account - placeholder values like 'your-username' will cause 404 errors.

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: Whether the repository is private. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction. Changing visibility may also be restricted by organization policies.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.description: Archives the repository (making it read-only) or unarchives it.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.description: Enables the GitHub Wiki.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.description: URL of the project's homepage.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.description: Enables GitHub Issues for this repository.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Repository visibility. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Brief summary of the repository.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.description: Designates this repository as a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.description: Enables GitHub Projects. Note: Organization settings might override this and cause an error if projects are disabled organization-wide.

inputs.name: allow_forking
inputs.description: Allows private forks of this repository. IMPORTANT: This setting can ONLY be changed for organization-owned repositories. Attempting to set this for user-owned repositories will result in a 422 error. Omit this field if you don't want to change the forking setting.

inputs.name: default_branch
inputs.description: Name of the default branch.

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.description: Enables auto-merge for pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_merge_commit
inputs.description: Allows merging pull requests via merge commits.

inputs.name: allow_rebase_merge
inputs.description: Allows rebase-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_squash_merge
inputs.description: Allows squash-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: merge_commit_title
inputs.description: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'. CONSTRAINT: If set to 'PR_TITLE', merge_commit_message must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. If set to 'MERGE_MESSAGE', merge_commit_message must be blank/unset.

inputs.name: allow_update_branch
inputs.description: Allows updating a pull request's head branch from the base branch if not strictly required for merging.

inputs.name: merge_commit_message
inputs.description: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'PR_TITLE', or 'BLANK'. CONSTRAINT: If merge_commit_title is 'PR_TITLE', this must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. Cannot be set when merge_commit_title is 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.

inputs.name: delete_branch_on_merge
inputs.description: Automatically deletes head branches after merging pull requests.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_title
inputs.description: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_message
inputs.description: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: web_commit_signoff_required
inputs.description: Requires contributors to sign off on web-based commits.

inputs.name: use_squash_pr_title_as_default
inputs.description: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. If true, squash merge commits use PR title; otherwise, commit message.

inputs.name: security__and__analysis__secret__scanning__status
inputs.description: Manages secret scanning. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

inputs.name: security__and__analysis__advanced__security__status
inputs.description: Manages GitHub Advanced Security. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

inputs.name: security__analysis__secret__scanning__push__protection__status
inputs.description: Manages secret scanning push protection. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

requiredInputs.name: repo

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: ACTUAL GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real, existing account - placeholder values like 'your-username' will cause 404 errors.

name: Update a repository ruleset
description: Updates an existing repository ruleset, identified by `ruleset_id` for a given repository, allowing partial updates to its configuration such as name, target, enforcement, bypass actors, conditions, and rules.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The new name for the ruleset.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: rules
inputs.description: String representing rules to enforce. Each rule has a `type` and optional `parameters` (e.g., `[{'type': 'creation'}, {'type': 'pull_request', 'parameters': {'required_approving_review_count': 2}}]`). Accepts both JSON format with double quotes or Python list syntax with single quotes. Refer to GitHub API documentation for available rule types and their parameters.

inputs.name: target
inputs.description: The target of the ruleset (e.g., branch or tag).

inputs.name: ruleset_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the existing ruleset to be updated.

inputs.name: enforcement
inputs.description: Specifies the enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is ignored), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (allows testing rules before enforcement - GitHub Enterprise only).

inputs.name: bypass_actors
inputs.description: String representing actors that can bypass ruleset. Each actor specifies `actor_id`, `actor_type` (`RepositoryRole`, `Team`, `Integration`, `OrganizationAdmin`), and `bypass_mode` (`always`, `pull_request`). Accepts both JSON format with double quotes or Python list syntax with single quotes. Example: `[{'actor_id': 1, 'actor_type': 'RepositoryRole', 'bypass_mode': 'always'}]`.

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__exclude
inputs.description: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to exclude. The ruleset will not apply if any of these patterns match.

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__include
inputs.description: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to include for the ruleset to apply. `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` targets the default branch; `~ALL` targets all branches.

requiredInputs.name: repo
requiredInputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: owner
requiredInputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

requiredInputs.name: ruleset_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique identifier of the existing ruleset to be updated.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: The new name for the ruleset.

optionalInputs.name: rules
optionalInputs.description: String representing rules to enforce. Each rule has a `type` and optional `parameters` (e.g., `[{'type': 'creation'}, {'type': 'pull_request', 'parameters': {'required_approving_review_count': 2}}]`). Accepts both JSON format with double quotes or Python list syntax with single quotes. Refer to GitHub API documentation for available rule types and their parameters.

optionalInputs.name: target
optionalInputs.description: The target of the ruleset (e.g., branch or tag).

optionalInputs.name: enforcement
optionalInputs.description: Specifies the enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is ignored), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (allows testing rules before enforcement - GitHub Enterprise only).

optionalInputs.name: bypass_actors
optionalInputs.description: String representing actors that can bypass ruleset. Each actor specifies `actor_id`, `actor_type` (`RepositoryRole`, `Team`, `Integration`, `OrganizationAdmin`), and `bypass_mode` (`always`, `pull_request`). Accepts both JSON format with double quotes or Python list syntax with single quotes. Example: `[{'actor_id': 1, 'actor_type': 'RepositoryRole', 'bypass_mode': 'always'}]`.

optionalInputs.name: conditions__ref__name__exclude
optionalInputs.description: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to exclude. The ruleset will not apply if any of these patterns match.

optionalInputs.name: conditions__ref__name__include
optionalInputs.description: Ref names or patterns (fnmatch-style globs) to include for the ruleset to apply. `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` targets the default branch; `~ALL` targets all branches.

name: Update a repository webhook
description: Updates the URL, content type, secret, SSL verification, events, or active status for an existing repository webhook, specified by `owner`, `repo`, and `hook_id`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Set `true` if active and sending notifications, `false` otherwise.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: List of events to trigger the webhook. GitHub REPLACES the full event list when this is sent — omit (leave unset) to preserve the existing subscription. Use `add_events`/`remove_events` for incremental changes. See GitHub webhook event payloads docs for available event types.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the webhook (found in `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a delivery).

inputs.name: add_events
inputs.description: Events to add to current subscriptions.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.description: New URL for webhook payload delivery.

inputs.name: remove_events
inputs.description: Events to remove from current subscriptions.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.description: Optional secret for HMAC digest of delivery signature headers (see GitHub docs).

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.description: Media type for serializing payloads ('json' or 'form').

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.description: Set to '1' to disable SSL verification (insecure), '0' to enable.

name: Update a team
description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a GitHub organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization's name (case-insensitive). This is a required path parameter.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: New display name for the team.

inputs.name: privacy
inputs.description:
The desired privacy level for the team. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `privacy` intact. When a team is nested, the `privacy` for parent teams cannot be `secret`.
  - For a non-nested team:
    - `secret`: Visible only to organization owners and members of this team.
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).
  - For a parent or child team:
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The team's unique slug identifier (typically URL-friendly version of team name). This is a required path parameter.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: **Deprecated**. Default permission (pull, push, or admin) for new repositories added to this team. This may be removed in future versions.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: New team description; an empty string clears the existing description.

inputs.name: parent_team_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier (ID) of an existing team to set as this team's parent. This establishes a nested team structure. To remove an existing parent team relationship and make this a top-level team, explicitly pass `null` for this field (e.g., by setting this parameter to Python `None`). If this parameter is not provided in the request, the current parent team, if any, remains unchanged.

inputs.name: notification_setting
inputs.description:
The notification setting the team has chosen. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `notification_setting` intact.
  - `notifications_enabled`: Team members receive notifications when the team is @mentioned.
  - `notifications_disabled`: No one receives notifications for team @mentions.

requiredInputs.name: org
requiredInputs.description: The organization's name (case-insensitive). This is a required path parameter.

requiredInputs.name: team_slug
requiredInputs.description: The team's unique slug identifier (typically URL-friendly version of team name). This is a required path parameter.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: New display name for the team.

optionalInputs.name: privacy
optionalInputs.description:
The desired privacy level for the team. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `privacy` intact. When a team is nested, the `privacy` for parent teams cannot be `secret`.
  - For a non-nested team:
    - `secret`: Visible only to organization owners and members of this team.
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).
  - For a parent or child team:
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).

optionalInputs.name: permission
optionalInputs.description: **Deprecated**. Default permission (pull, push, or admin) for new repositories added to this team. This may be removed in future versions.

optionalInputs.name: description
optionalInputs.description: New team description; an empty string clears the existing description.

optionalInputs.name: parent_team_id
optionalInputs.description: The unique identifier (ID) of an existing team to set as this team's parent. This establishes a nested team structure. To remove an existing parent team relationship and make this a top-level team, explicitly pass `null` for this field (e.g., by setting this parameter to Python `None`). If this parameter is not provided in the request, the current parent team, if any, remains unchanged.

optionalInputs.name: notification_setting
optionalInputs.description:
The notification setting the team has chosen. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `notification_setting` intact.
  - `notifications_enabled`: Team members receive notifications when the team is @mentioned.
  - `notifications_disabled`: No one receives notifications for team @mentions.

inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Name of the branch to protect; wildcard characters are not allowed (use GraphQL API for wildcard support).

inputs.name: lock_branch
inputs.description: If `True`, sets the branch to read-only, preventing pushes.

inputs.name: enforce_admins
inputs.description: Enforces configured restrictions for repository administrators. Set to `True` to enforce for admins, or `None`/`null` to disable.

inputs.name: allow_deletions
inputs.description: Allows deletion of the protected branch.

inputs.name: block_creations
inputs.description: If `True`, push restrictions also apply to creating matching new branches, unless pusher has explicit push access.

inputs.name: allow_force_pushes
inputs.description: Permits force pushes to the protected branch. Set to `True` to allow, `None`/`null` to block.

inputs.name: allow_fork_syncing
inputs.description: Allows users to pull upstream changes into their fork when the branch is locked.

inputs.name: restrictions__apps
inputs.description: App `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_users` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: restrictions__teams
inputs.description: Team `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_users`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: restrictions__users
inputs.description: User `login`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required_linear_history
inputs.description: Enforces linear commit history by preventing merge commits. Repository must allow squash or rebase merging.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__checks
inputs.description: List of status checks required to merge. Each check object needs a `context` string and optionally an `app_id` integer. **Mutually exclusive with `contexts`** - use one or the other, not both.

inputs.name: app_id
inputs.description: The optional ID of the GitHub App that must provide this check.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: The name of the required status check.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__strict
inputs.description: Require branches to be up to date with the base branch before merging; if `True`, merges are blocked if the head branch is behind the base branch.

inputs.name: required_conversation_resolution
inputs.description: Requires all conversations on code to be resolved before merging a pull request.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__contexts
inputs.description: **Deprecated**: List of status check contexts required to merge. If set by a GitHub App, future checks must come from that app. Use `checks` for finer control. **Mutually exclusive with `checks`** - use one or the other, not both.

inputs.name: required__pull__request__reviews__dismiss__stale__reviews
inputs.description: If `True`, new commits dismiss previous review approvals on a pull request branch.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__approving__review__count
inputs.description: Number of approving reviews (0-6) required to merge a pull request; 0 disables.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__apps
inputs.description: App `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__teams
inputs.description: Team `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__users
inputs.description: User `login`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__apps
inputs.description: App `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__teams
inputs.description: Team `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__users
inputs.description: User `login`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__require__code__owner__reviews
inputs.description: If `True`, pull requests affecting files with code owners must be reviewed by them.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__require__last__push__approval
inputs.description: If `True`, the most recent push must be approved by someone other than the pusher.

inputs.name: repo

inputs.name: auto_trigger_checks
inputs.description: A list of preference objects as a JSON string for configuring automatic CheckSuite creation for specific GitHub Apps. Each object in the list must contain an `app_id` (integer: the ID of the GitHub App) and a `setting` (boolean: `true` to enable automatic triggers for that app on code push, `false` to disable). Example: '[{"app_id": 12345, "setting": true}]'

name: Update status check protection
description: Updates required status checks for a branch, optionally requiring it to be up-to-date before merging.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch (required, cannot be null or empty). Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, please use the GraphQL API.

inputs.name: checks
inputs.description: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. Each check object should have a `context` (string) and an optional `app_id` (integer). Example: `[{"context": "continuous-integration/jenkins", "app_id": 12345}]`

inputs.name: app_id
inputs.description: The optional ID of the GitHub App that must provide this check.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: The name of the required status check.

inputs.name: strict
inputs.description: Specifies whether to require branches to be up to date before merging.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: **Deprecated**: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. If any of these checks were recently set by a particular GitHub App, they will be required to come from that app in the future for the branch to merge. For more fine-grained control, use `checks` instead of `contexts`. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings.

optionalInputs.name: app_id

optionalInputs.name: context

name: Update the authenticated user
description: Updates the authenticated user's GitHub profile; a new public email must be verified, and existing private emails remain private even if specified.

inputs.name: bio
inputs.description: The new short biography for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing bio.

inputs.name: blog
inputs.description: The new URL for the user's blog. Provide `null` to clear the existing blog URL. For example, 'https://github.blog'.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The new full name for the user. For example, 'Mona Lisa Octocat'.

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: The user's new publicly visible email address. This email must be verified on the user's GitHub account. If the provided email is not verified or not set to public, the update for this field might be ignored.

inputs.name: company
inputs.description: The new company affiliation for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing company. For example, 'GitHub'.

inputs.name: hireable
inputs.description: Indicates whether the user is available for hire. Set to `true` if available, or `false` if not.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: The new geographical location of the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing location. For example, 'San Francisco'.

inputs.name: twitter_username
inputs.description: The user's new Twitter username (without the leading '@'). Provide `null` to clear the existing Twitter username. For example, 'github'.

optionalInputs.description: The new short biography for the user. Provide `null` to clear the existing bio.

description: Tool to update your status on GitHub. Use when you need to set or clear your GitHub profile status with an emoji, message, and availability indicator.

inputs.name: emoji
inputs.description: The emoji to represent your status. Can either be a native Unicode emoji or an emoji name with colons, e.g., :rocket:, :smiley:. If omitted, any existing emoji will be cleared.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: A short description of your current status. Maximum 80 characters. If omitted, any existing message will be cleared.

inputs.name: expiresAt
inputs.description: If set, the user status will not be shown after this date. Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g., 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z). If omitted, the status will persist indefinitely.

inputs.name: organizationId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the organization whose members will be allowed to see the status. If omitted, the status will be publicly visible to everyone. Use this to limit status visibility to organization members only.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This value will be returned in the response and can be used for request tracking or idempotency.

inputs.name: limitedAvailability
inputs.description: Whether this status should indicate you are not fully available on GitHub (e.g., you are away or busy). When true, shows a busy indicator. Defaults to false if omitted.

name: Upload an analysis as sarif data
description: Uploads a gzipped and Base64 encoded SARIF file to a GitHub repository for a specific commit and reference; use `checkout_uri` if SARIF paths are absolute.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The full Git reference for the analysis, formatted as `refs/heads/<branch name>`, `refs/tags/<tag>`, `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, or `refs/pull/<number>/head`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: sarif
inputs.description: A Base64 encoded string of a gzipped SARIF file. For more information, see '[SARIF support for code scanning](https://docs.github.com/code-security/secure-coding/sarif-support-for-code-scanning)'.

inputs.name: validate
inputs.description: Specifies whether the uploaded SARIF file should be validated against the code scanning specifications. This is useful for integrators to ensure their SARIF files are correctly processed and rendered by GitHub.

inputs.name: tool_name
inputs.description: Name of the tool that generated the analysis. GitHub defaults this to "API" if omitted. A tool GUID in the SARIF can be used for filtering alerts.

inputs.name: commit_sha
inputs.description: The SHA of the commit to which the analysis you are uploading relates. Must be a 40-character Git SHA.

inputs.name: started_at
inputs.description: The timestamp indicating when the analysis run began, in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: checkout_uri
inputs.description: The base URI of the checkout directory as it appears in the SARIF file. This property is used to convert absolute file paths to relative paths, enabling alerts to be correctly mapped to their location in the repository.
